rows.text: I love to read and I am always looking for good mysteries and science fiction.  Amazon.com and Waldenbooks both listed Dennis Lehane on my account under recommendations.  I read the first few pages of three of his books and bought them, now I wish I had read a little further.  Lehane gets  rave reviews from quite a few sources and nowhere in those reviews have I  seen one word about Lehane being ignorant, disrespectful and downright  prejudiced, which he is.  You see Lehane refers to black people with that  old ugly word, yes the 'n' word.  He is consistent and most of the  characters in this book use that ugly archaic term.  It must make things  easier for Lehane in terms of characther development, look at all the time  he saves in fleshing out a character by simply describing them as a nigger.  Well Lehane, I am Black and definitely not a nigger.  I am a consumer, a  consumer who loves to spend money on books and one who will never again  enrich you coffers with even ten cents of my money.  If anyone is looking  for a detective to love try teaming up with Elvis Cole, a series of books  by  Robert Crais.  If you see people as people, good and bad, don't buy  books from this author who sees people in terms of color and if the color  isn't white then the person couldn't possibly be a good worthwhile human  being.
rows.title: Lehane, please join us in the 21st century.

rows.text: I could not get pass p. 78 when the racial slurs became too much for me as an African-American. His reference to a young black male, once on p. 78 & twice on p.79 as "N***er kid or other words the N word. I had notice this type of racial slurs in most of his books and thought I could get pass it. I had not read any Stephen King books in more than 10 years and decided to try this one. I will never again read another. I realized that I am not one of his intended audience but some of us do read.
rows.title: No more Stephen King for me

rows.text:
I hoped Id never have to write a bad review, but here it is. This coffee maker sucks dick. Ill start with the pot. Its a bullshit pot, because its got this stupid plastic lid which pours fine at first but blocks the last half cup of water! To get all the water out you have to flip the pot completely upside down so the opening points straight downwards, at which point the remaining half cup of water just goes everywhere. Like what!? Why!? Surely its not that hard to make a pot that can pour all its contents right?! It doesnt help that the hole for the water is hard to reach and really small. Id love to see a video of somebody filling the water on this thing properly, cause i dont believe its possible.
Did none of the engineers test this thing before approving it? Its seriously ridiculous how bad it is.
Heres why I'm ashamed. I read all these reviews and brushed them off thinking "these guys are fucking morons they just dont know how to pour a coffee pot". No, I was wrong, or I am also a moron like them. This thing is seriously stupid.
The rest of the feature work fine, sure. But no Mr. Coffee, you still get only 1 star because every single morning Im reminded that Im the idiot that bought a coffee maker where i cant even pour all the coffee out of the pot, and cant fill the fricking thing without making a mess. I dont care that everything else works, the only thing I needed was a basic-ass coffee maker. I hereby boycott you and all your products FOR LIFE
rows.title: WTF how could you mess up a coffee maker this bad!?

rows.text:
A few points to further enlighten the above reviewer with the self-proclaimed world class palate, self-styled biting wit, and self-affirmed firm deathgrip on logic and reason:

1. 'Truffle Essence' is not an actual infusion of real truffles of any sort into anything. Almost no 'truffle' products contain any real truffles, fresh or otherwise. No dried ones, no ground ones, no back-alley knockoffs, no mushrooms that maybe went to high school with a real truffle and sat at the same lunch table because they were 'cool with each other'. Nothing. This product is no exception. There are no truffles in this. At all. Your tongue is a liar.

It does, however, contain synthetically made organosulfurs which are produced both for industrial reasons and to mimic real truffles for rubes wanting to feel fancy. Doping a bottle of slick, oily, cloying pseudo-sriracha with lab-produced pretend truffle farts is the functional equivalent of pissing in a bottle of Sweet Baby Ray's while saying a prayer and calling it Hot Jesus Juice. It's still just hot piss in a bottle.

2. It does not taste 'exactly like black truffles'. Or any truffles. At all.

It does taste exactly like formaldehyde and kerosene swirled around with a bit of gas station kimchi that's been warming down the front of a hobo's pants. How do I know? To give this comment validity, I took the liberty of mixing up that exact concoction, then I went to down the train yard and asked a hobo to warm it up for me right on his swampy, fetid hobo taint. After I gave him 20 bucks and a bottle of pruno, I served it - slathered across some gorgeous hand cut double fried Belgian pommes frites - to a panel of judges, all of whom graduated at the top of their respective classes at the Culinary Institute of America. Right next to it I served a plate of the same frites with TRUFF™ on them. Double blind, Jack. Verdict? They all preferred the warm hobo taint sauce - even the ones who were given a napalm palate cleanser and a shot of Hot Jesus Juice in between. Uncanny.

Anyone who has ever actually tasted a real truffle (clearly not you) would know the difference in an instant. Truffle flavors ARE subtle, despite your ALL CAPS protestations to the contrary. The aroma is strong and heady, but the taste and finish is indescribably complex, earthy, and somewhere between subtly sweet and delicately funky.

In stark contrast, the chemical cocktail that this hackjob pisspot 'truffle' knockoff and every other hackjob pisspot knockoff on the market try to mimic is only one note in a complex symphony of flavors, and it's a screeching, grinding, blood curdling dissonant note if played on its own. It's like someone yanked an F sharp out of Haydn, stuck it in a Bro Country ballad about pickup trucks and country poon, and called it genius. It's not. It's Florida Georgia Line. It tastes like Bro Country. And hobo taint. Not sure there's a real difference between them.

3. You've eaten at The French Laundry? Cool story, bro. What'd you get, the chicken tender plate with extra Becky sauce? It's story I imagine you tell far too often, and with far too much fake humblebraggart enthusiasm for your acquaintances to call you anything but the insufferable twat you clearly are, as they halfheartedly giggle at your terrible jokes and glance over and over at their watches, their souls screaming for that hour of sweet release when they can make a hurried but polite exit from FoodieToTheMax headquarters and wash that diesel taste from their mouths.

Myself, I've actually eaten at many restaurants with varying numbers of Michelin stars. I've had real truffles. I actually do know good food.

I've had bucket list meals in dozens of cities. I've been wowed by Jean Georges, disappointed by Mario Batali, and floored by simple street food that cost less than four dollars but destroyed them both. I once accidentally walked into Aureole in NYC thinking it was just a lame theatre district cocktail bar, and wound up eating all seven courses of their tasting menu in a six dollar football tee. It was a grand time. Laughs, libations, and good times were enjoyed by all. I've seen much of the food world and have a true appreciation for things that actually taste good. This is not one of them.

In short - If you really do think this insipid glop is anything short of uninspired curdled monkey piss in a ten dollar can, then I can't help you. And that's sad, because you sorely need it.

You know what is actually delicious? Crystal hot sauce. Chefs use it extensively. But you knew that right, super foodie? You lambasted some poor commenter above for liking it. Guess what? Her palate is more refined than yours, BY FAR, Mister French Laundry.

Enjoy the ride in your extra long douche canoe. You still have a lot of paddling to do. Hurry up, I hear banjos.
rows.title: Like drinking literal garbage.

rows.text:
The below review I posted months ago.

The game has been updated a couple times since then. They added new weapon variants, some new characters, and weapons.

To sum it up. This game is still terrible.

Because it's such a terribly buggy game that freezes often in multiplayer especially against a specific enemy type called the kett and when one enemy called the fiend is in play. This happens so often most players know about this issue. An issue that I can guarantee will never be fixed. As I said, the buggy nature of this game resulted in my being unable to load the game. I only put about 50+ hours into the boring and terrible single-player, but I figured I may still force myself to play the terrible multiplayer. So I deleted and redownloaded the game. Now I reserved this game (big mistake) with some of the bonus material. After redownloading the game, this "bonus" material was no longer available. As a result, all my previous saves were nullified and would not load. So, this meant I had to start the single player again. I got about 15 hours in until I couldn't take it anymore. The cheesy lines, the bad graphics, the terrible gameplay, the boring exploration, the clumsy six-wheeled car/tank thing.

But I kept playing the multiplayer (MP) and it is just awful. From basic game mechanics to gameplay to reliability to playability, the game is just awful. I often find myself repeating the same thing out loud everytime I play: "I HATE THIS F------ING GAME!" I say this phrase so often I then constantly ask myself why I keep playing it. The only answer I can think of is that I hope it might get better when I level up this character or this gun. But deep down I know that isn't going to happen. The game is just so terrible at it's root, nothing will make it better. It's like a bad vegan burger patty I once had. It's supposed to taste as good as a real burger but it didn't. No amount of cheese, condiment, or spices I added would make this taste better:
- weapons are weak
- aiming is terrible
- powers are weak/unreliable
- movement is laggy, clumsy, slow
- there is built in lag, meaning after everything you do: use a power, fire a weapon, dash, there is a pause before you can do anything else
- terrible cover system
- ledge grabbing is overused, forced and clumsy
- enemy AI is unbalanced and overpowered compared to playable characters

Consider this: these "geniuses" behind andromeda had to release patches that weakened enemies and increased weapon damage. But just to be the jerks that they are, the reduced damage or some feature of some of the favored weapons and increased damage of some of the terrible weapons. Remember the vegan burger? Terrible is still terrible.

They came up with a variant to all the weapons called a "siphon" that give you back health based on the damage your weapon causes. Specifically a small fraction (around 3%) of the damage you cause returns as health. At least that's what the description says. It doesn't work that way though. I noticed in ME3 that bioware doesn't know how math works. You might pick a power perk that increases base damage by 50% but in reality it only increases it by 30%. Use a siphon weapon on weaker enemies and you get zero to nearly zero health back. It doesn't matter if you kill several base enemies if your health is low, your still screwed and will die is an enemy even looks at you in the wrong way. Use that same siphon weapon on a more powerful enemy and you get significantly more health back. Mind you significantly more than zero isn't a large amount. This makes no sense. The damage a weapon outputs is the same regardless of you you shoot. But that's just how andromeda rolls. It's asinine. It pushes agendas. It takes the joy from gameplay by making things weak, clumsy, slow, lagged, corny, lame, and boring..... all by design.

These people surely never played ME3 multiplayer and if they did, they must have been terrible at it. They took everything that made ME3 multiplayer great and ruined it. Examples:
- Great weapons that were well balanced and had character (i.e. Javelin sniper, M99 Saber, Particle Rifle, Cerberus Harrier) are non-existent in this game or there is a version of the weapon in andromeda that just terrible.
- Great characters that were well balanced and had character... same description as above
- cover grab (grab someone in cover and beat instakill them)
- Cobra missiles (one shot kill a group of enemies no questions asked)

Yet they took things that were hated in ME3 and forced their use in andromeda
- automated cover system
- clumsy useless auto targeting system
- very long load times
- the way the game lags when a new player joins the lobby mid-game (Yes, these "genuises" actually made this happen in every game you play, even if you are alone in a private lobby, the game will freeze and lag for a moment early in round one. Not just at the beginning, no. It happens inconveniently after you've already been engaged by the enemy).
- Some of the least liked weapons in ME3 have for some reason found their way into andromeda. Whereas the aforementioned beloved weapons are no where to be found.

Stupid, just plain absolutely stupid game design. I have a deep loathing for the people behind this game. I tried to get into it. I forced myself to play it. I have put in now hours, but dozen and dozens of days worth of time into this game and just find myself hating it more and more.

Most of my friends have either stopped playing this game or gone back to ME3.

If I start a lobby, I would end up sitting for 10, 15, 20 minutes with no one joining. If I start a game on my own, I could potentially go through the entire mission solo. If you paid attention to anything of what I've been saying, you can imagine how miserable and frustrating playing this game solo can be. If I try to join another lobby, it is a guarantee that I will load into the match after the enemies in the first wave have already spawned and I will probably be killed before even loading in. I will likely be paired with rookie players that are just terrible, or in some rare cases, with the dozen or two andromeda players that only do three things in life: eat, sleep, play andromeda. I've spoken with some of these guys, and they have all their weapons and characters maxed out. They are not all good players, in fact I am better than most of them. Fan boys will say I hate thing game because I'm not good at it. Well, I have been ranked in the top 30 since the game came out. In 90% of the games I play, I am ranked #1 in points. I've had many players compliment me on how well I play or hate on me for how well I play. That doesn't matter. I may be good at the game. But I still hate it. I still find it frustrating and asinine.

If there was an video game award for most disappointing sequel to a hit game, the maker of andromeda should receive this award along with a termination letter preventing them from ever working on video games again.
______________________________________________________________________________________________________________

There are plenty of the reviews about the single-player which boils down to this: It's not horrible, but far from good.

With that being said, this review is a hard look at the multi-player of ME:A. For my background. I'm an older gamer. I've played the original Mass Effect, ME2, and enjoyed the story line when I was younger. Years later though, I find that games I loved 10 or 15 years ago are boring and tedious now. I just don't have the patience for them as I did before. But multiplayer I love to play. Fast paced and action packed and of course very competitive. I never gave ME3's mp a second thought until I realized it can have an impact on the controversial ME3 ending. So I started playing it, and fell in love with it. I stopped playing most other games. I was enthralled with ME3 mp. Years of playing and I still learned new strategies and skills that I wasn't aware of before. It was a thoroughly enjoyable, yet far from perfect mp game. It seemed clear that ME3's MP was an after thought that didn't get a great deal of focus until EA or Bioware or whoever started to realize how many gamers were still playing the MP. So, it is for this reason mainly that I sunk $60+ hard earned, money I really couldn't afford, into Mass Effect Andromeda only for it to become an infamously terrible game according to many reviews. Worse, I now see it for sale at nearly half the reserved price. I actually wasn't going to reserve the game but many online gamer friends I met as a result of ME3's mp urged me to get Andromeda so we can all play together. Incidentally, most of those friends no longer play Andromeda. So, if you enjoyed ME3's mp here is a list I compiled of why ME:A mp is a worse comparison.

10 Things I HATE about Mass Effect: Andromeda Multiplayer (btw I LOVED ME:3 MP)

1. Weak arse weapons. Yes there are some ultra-rares that do moderate to good damage.
Though a majority of the weapons are truly garbage. You can get around this by using power based characters.
But for example, an UR weapon from ME:3 that I loved, the M99 Saber which was pretty good even at level 1, you won't find in Andormeda MP. I could, with the AR damage mod and a scope, snipe and one-shot head-shot kill most weak to moderate enemies.
In this shite game, the Black Widow sniper rifle often fails to kill a base enemy on silver! There's a sniper called the
isharey that has something like with 2000 damage in a single shot. I used this sniper with an infiltrator character thus giving it near
100%+ damage when in cloak. I've head-shot base enemies in gold and it failed to kill the enemy. It's truly ridiculous how
weak these weapons are. They even added a patch that increased weapon damage and weakened enemies because the balance was so bad upon release. This helped with game play somewhat but like everything else in the game, it's still lacking.

2. I seriously feel like there were people developing or designing this game that are truly arseholes who just wanted
to ruin the fun or the joy in the game with some asinine idiotic ideas that were unfortunately implemented in this game.
Two of which are the ledge grabbing BS and walking. Yes, for reasons that are beyond my comprehension, perhaps to immitate this stupid design from other MP games, but now you can walk in ME andromeda. Yes, because being able to walk as opposed to run was something
I'm sure gamers from ME3 were hoping for. You can press down on the directional control stick to run, but why should that even be an option?! Furthermore, running is so slow in this game to the point of frustration. The jet pack is an okay idea although somewhat gimmicky. I was worried when I heard they were adding this feature that they would add some gimmick like ledge grabs, just to push their obnoxious agendas (and that is what ruined this game to me,
the way they force agenda's into the game whether it be game mechanics or political). Sure enough, in the single player,
you will find yourself becoming friggen mario from mario brothers hopping and jumping here and there to get to a stupid objective to activate this console to go to this area to activate another console to go back to that first area to re-activate another console, hippity hoppity jumpity whoo. I dare say it's worse that mario brothers.
I can barely tolerate the constant unnecessary jumping. But what "grinds my gears" is this stupid idea of placing literally
every jump point, every ledge, everything just out of reach of being able to be jumped ON and instead you have to rely
on the stupid buggy game mechanics to allow your character to grab the ledge and pull you up onto the surface of whatever
it was you were trying to jump on. So if it's a multiplayer game, and you need to jump onto the upper floor of an area to get away
from enemies shooting at you or to get to an objective or downed teammate, there are times where no matter how much or hard you mash the controller button, you seem to be jumping just out of reach or your character fails to grab the ledge. But there is another factor that
makes things worse but I will get to that in number 3. Mass effect fan boys will deny this is a problem but I've often seen
players try again and again to jump onto a ledge area that they or I have been able to "jump" (and by jump I mean hope the
stupid game mechanic allows you to grab a ledge and pull you up) onto before. Whichever shiteheads thought it would be a good idea
to add WALKING and LEDGE GRABBING I hope are fired from their job.

3. Remember in ME3 how you would be running away from a banshee chasing your or your trying to get to a downed teammate and your
character kept slapping up against the wall or any object you come close to trying to take cover. The game mechanics
in ME3 were clumsy, yet the game was still fairly enjoyable. But the cover system mechanic was frustrating. So much so, that I eventually learned to play ME3 without using much cover. Instead I would stand near an obstacle and not push the button to take cover. If I need to take cover, I'll just stand behind a wall or corner. My shields will come back up in a couple seconds
as well as my powers and I'll be good to go. Well, some arse decided to create an automatic cover system. See? Like I said,
they push their agenda. Now ANYTIME you approach ANYTHING your character will try to take cover behind it. Trying to jump to
a higher area, well you will often find yourself fighting with the game mechanics trying to make you take cover on that wall you are
trying to jump over. I cannot express to you how frustrating it is to try and get away from a group of enemies coming after you as you try to escape by jumping up over a wall with cover only to find for some reason you can't get
high enough, even though you've done it several times before in the same damned mission, whilst watching your character idiotically
taking cover against the wall in front of you, and the enemies are shooting you from behind. I've even had my character try and take cover against an enemy! The jumping is completely random. Sometimes you get high enough, sometimes you won't. There's one map in particular where sometimes I can jump completely over a wall with cover and other times I can only jump high enough for the stupid computer to let me grab the ledge and pull myself over, then there's times where I can't even jump high enough to do that. It's one hard press of the button and yet I can get three different outcomes! I hate the cover sytem! There's a map called "Icebreaker" with ice/snow on the ground. The ground is therefore very irregular in some placed. As a result, your character will idiotically try to take cover from the small dips in the ground. Since you obviously can't take cover behind a few inches of ice, your character will hesitate and you have to jump over the irregularity to avoid the stupid game mechanics getting confused trying to take cover.

But it gets worse! In ME3, like I said, it doesn't take long for your shields to recharge. But once again the arseholes strike back with
their shite agendas, because in this game if you want your shields to recharge almost like it did in ME3, you HAVE to take cover. If you don't,
it could take as much as 5 seconds or more for your shields to START to recharge, and even then it does it slowly at first and builds up in speed
right around the time it's fully charged. Five seconds is a lifetime doing a gold match surrounded by enemies, whilst having to jam down on the controller stick so you can sprint and pushing the stick forward for you to go. I've had my xbox one for years. Played multiple games on the xbox one (including ME3!!!) and never had the problem of my controller sticking. Now it does. The stick has been damaged because out of frustration because the characters in this game are so damned SLOW!
Even with sprint! You'll find players constantly spamming the jet pack so they can slide a few feet forward more quickly. But just like ME3's few characters that had the ability to dash forward, backward, or to the sides, there is still a pause before you can do it again. Thus, a player just running behind you, can nearly keep up despite the dash. So it's just a futile attempt to use the jet pack to move further. It's just too damned slow. Why is this a problem. Well if you are getting shot at by the enemy, you will die before you can get away or find cover. It's incredibly frustrating.

4. Lag is still an issue. With the most recent patch, you can see the connection strength of the host but I find that baffling. I have my xbox one directly connected. I have connection speeds as fast as 80+ mbps with uploads as high as 20mpbs and pings as low as 15ms. YET, if I start a lobby, it will take FOREVER to find any players, but if I go to a quick search I can find a team in a second. In ME3 I rarely had that problem. I can host all day and no one really complained of lag issues.
But in this game, even if I find a host with a strong connection, I often experience lag. Sometimes it's simply game breaking, other times it just damned annoying. You would think that when adding things like jet packs, sprinting, faster paced combat, etc. this might give the developers pause to think "should we add dedicated servers and if not, we should at least consider this added stuff will this cause frame rate drops,
or other issues for non-hosting players?" No of course not. That's not what arseholes do.

5. The characters in multiplayer look ridiculous and will say some of the most cheesiest things like: "Skidaddle!", "coolio", "gracias" (in the most non-spanish accent possible), or "friendly neighborhood hydra". Also, most characters seem to have a complete lack of understanding about how their shields work or just have a plain disdain for their shields. Then there's the alcoholic agenda "Next rounds on me" or "drinks or on me". Some of them look like someone decided to take some sporting equipment armor and wear that along with a motorcycle helmet,
a plastic looking gun with some "wear" on the paint scheme, some denim jeans or leather to go into battle. Seriously it's kind of laughable. And to push their social agenda, there HAS to be an equal or greater number of female characters. So get ready to hear some deep sounding krogan females voices. I don't even consider myself a misogynist. I remember ME3 having a fair amount of female characters
most of which were my favorite (i.e. alliance infiltrator, N7 Fury) but in this game the female additions feel forced. To make matters worse, you have to unlock cards via purchase of money or credits, that work completely through chance to unlock additional skill points to fully upgrade your character. This doesn't take too long but for the ultra rare characters, this could take months if not years
depending how much time (or money) you have to invest in playing this game and just plain luck. What they should have done is require the player to reset the level and level up certain characters a number of times
to eventually unlock their full potential. In doing so, you kinda have to earn the fully unlocked version of that character as well as learn how to play that character well. But nope, the card lottery is what they want.
And I know why! I know why the multiplayer started out so hard, why the guns were so weak, why the gameplay is kinda awful. They hoped that you will give in to your frustrations and just start spending your money to buy cards to get better
weapons and characters. I refuse! In ME3 they used this lottery system as well. It can get frustrating at times, but there were so many great guns and characters, it was worth the wait and effort. This system can be defined as a variable-positive reinforcer. It's kind of how a manipulative significant other who generally was a terrible boyfriend/girlfriend, but you stay with them due to those seemingly random intermittent periods of good times in the relationship. It's part of the behaviorists psychology of behavior reinforcement and why gambling can be such a strong addiction for some.

6. Aiming. Remember there were certain enemies in ME3 that were just damned annoying and invoked your hatred because they were so damned hard to target and moved so fast, like the nemesis and phantom from cerberus? Well, the arseholes from ME andromeda must have thought you loved those characters because now most of the enemies run, dodge, sprint, duck, shuffle, hop and do all kinds of moves to avoid getting hit by your powers and YES even your weapon.

I'm not kidding. I've played this game and fired a sniper rifle at an enemy facing away from me, only to see that enemy hop to the side just as I fired. The AI is that damned annoying. If they dodge weapon fire like this,
how many times do you think they dodge powers like throw and incinerate which still do the stupid arc and hit walls, ceilings, etc. rather than the enemy. If this wasn't bad enough, aiming has become extremely frustrating.
Especially if you are not hosting.

7. Certain weapon types, powers, and equipment from ME3 are no where to be found in Andromeda which I just hate. For example, the only weapon that comes close to the M99 I so loved from ME3 is a sniper called the vanquisher. Unfortunately,
another annoying thing about this game are the slow reload times. That's another issue for number 8. Most of the assault rifles in this game are absolutely terrible. Truly like I said most of the guns are. But powers like the recon mine are
nowhere to be found, grenade mechanics seem to defy logical physics and are thrown like they are extra bouncy fluffy nerf balls or land no where near where you intended. Worse, they added "targeting assist" with grenades that are hilariously unreliable. I've thrown sticky grenades at a hydra (one of the largest enemies) and watched as the grenade arc will curve left or right completely missing the enemy, even when the enemy is standing still. But if I throw the grenade at an enemy standing near or partially behind cover, the grenade will land on that cover, thus causing no damage. The one armor equipment I used with nearly every character in ME3 was the Geth scanner (which allows you to see enemies highlighted even behind walls) there's nothing like that here. Oh, they can most certainly create such a mod since in every mission when you get to the last three enemies they are highlighted or the fact that they have one character that uses this as a power. But an equipment mod that does this? Nope. I'm certain if you polled top players from ME3 Multiplayer and asked if they like this scanning mod, they would say yes.
There are few characters that have reliable crowd control powers other than the flamethrower that a couple characters have. Perhaps you can spam a bunch of grenades but like everything else, those can be weak as well.

8. Apparently people must have complained about how fast reloading weapons were was in ME3 and the arsholes wanted to remedy that. In ME3 you can almost cut the obnoxious, lazy, casual reload animation. Not in this game. Sometimes, even when you wait for the animation to finalize and you move, jump, use cover, use a power, or get blown by a gentle breeze, you have to friggen reload again. Sometimes even after patiently waiting to reload and waiting a little bit longer just in case of lag, after firing you weapon, your character will automatically start reloading again, even though you haven't emptied your clip!

9. The unusual randomness of everything makes me hate this game further. I can use a gun to headshot a base character in this game and it won't kill them leaving some health visible. In the same mission, using the same gun, on another base character, that shot might kill him.
Yet again, same gun, same base character, same mission, I can headshot and the entire health bar is gone, yet the character is still running around alive, even when I'm host. Most would instantly blame lag, but I've seen this happen as I'm hosting the game!

Powers will sometimes kill an enemy, sometimes they wont. Sometimes the power will work, sometimes it won't. Sometimes you set up a combo explosion, sometimes it just doesn't happen. There's a character called the asari sentinal that has a biotic shield that deflects enemy fire and some powers. If you have good timing,
you can bring up that shield and deflect up to 500% damage back at the enemy (of course this only scratches most enemies since they are faster, stronger, can jump higher, and move faster than most of the MP characters you can use). But after pressing the button the shield will go up then immediately go back down. Since I put myself out there depending on my shield to deflect weapon fire or a power, how frustrating is it you think to have that power start then just as immediately come off and I get hit or die? Another character, the human kinetistic has the power to
pull an enemy and throw them great distances. Wellllll LOL, sometimes the enemy gets pulled. Sometimes they don't. Sometimes when pull actually works as it should, throw doesn't work. Sometimes when pull properly works and throw properly works I can only throw the characters a few feet.
Sometimes! Sometimes when pull properly works, and throw properly works, and I can actually throw them seemingly off the map, the bastards come back and are shooting me again! Yes, even after throwing them seeming 1000 meters off the map and down a cliff, they somehow comeback!
I've seen other complain about this on other forums. ME3 was certainly not bug free and had it's issues but not as bad as this game.

10. The never powerful cobra RPG. In ME3 this was the GODSMACK of weapons. When you needed to clear every MOFO in the room no questions asked you can almost always rely on the cobra missile. Well guess what the andromeda arseholes have done?! In this game, I've used a cobra rpg on a atlas (Erm I mean Hydra)
only to see half it's armer health go down. Then a teammate will use their cobra rpg and the damned enemy is still alive with some health still there! It would take a third RPG to finish the thing off. It doesn't matter if you hit the "weak point" because like everything in this
damned game, everything is so buggy and random. I once used 5 cobra missiles to kill a fiend. I remember that was one of my most frustrating games because I lost the lead in point due to being instakilled by a fiend in wave 3 because none of my teammates were doing the mission except myself. Then every consecutive wave after
I was constantly stalked by every fiend that spawned. Despite using a fully upgraded character, gold level incindiary ammo, and a fully leveled rare smg, it took nearly a minute to kill one fiend. Then, literally as soon as I killed that one, another had spawned and charged me nearly picking me up for another instakill. I fired an rpg
it only took down a third of the health. I ran away to get enough distance to "aim" as best as can be done in this shit game, and hit the fiend in the face (weakspot) and it was STILL alive with a half of it's health. This was the extraction wave and the fiend was now sorrounded by enemies shooting at me, so I used another rpg at the floor
near the fiend and it failed to kill most of the enemies, even the base level enemies, and the fiend was STILL alive. So I used my fourth rpg and it looked like the fiend health was now completely gone but NOPE, it was still alive with what looked like an empty health bar and charging at me again. I said "screw it" and used my final rpg
to kill it. I mean, WTF?!?!? It was a gold match, but the hydra example I gave above was a silver match! Even if you factor in lag, the other teammate the took the second rpg shot at the hyrda was host! It two of my rpg's including his as the second shot to kill a hydra on silver?! And yes, I know there will be an andromeda fan boy who will announce
"I always kill a fiend with just one cobra rpg on gold" yeah well good for you, because I've rarely seen it happen when I do it or another teammate tries.

I continue to play this game because I've been in love with Mass Effect from the very first game and have spent countless hours playing ME3 multiplayer and had gotten damned good at it. Andromeda's multiplayer isn't terrible, but it is damned frustrating and annoying. It's really a disappointment and I spent my hard earned money on this game
and feel as though I should try to get something out of it, but damn. I just had to vent about the frustrations I have with this game.
rows.title: A Review for the Lover of ME3's Multiplayer

rows.text:
For fans of the older Halo games, and even the newer ones such as Halo: Reach, expecting something better or at least up to par than the previous Halo games, this game is not for you. Halo 4 consists of a poorly pieced together campaign with less "Halo goodness" and more of Cortana getting psycho bitchy at everyone, UNSC soldiers calling scientists "Eggheads", and Master Chief getting thrown around. The story line is a solid meh, and the easter eggs feel forced and unoriginal (for example they more often than not reference an easter egg from an older Halo game, or a RvB quote or something). Rather than Firefight, Halo 4 has a mode called "Spartan Ops", a 50-episode minigame where your multiplayer Spartan must repeat the same 5 missions over again 10 times in the same maps from the campaign, with slightly varying outcomes each episode (for example one episode, you might need to save someone on foot, and in another one you need to save someone in a warthog). A very pissy spartan commander chick yells at you through each episode.
The multiplayer has a few good changes, but mostly it feels dry and buggy. My internet connection isn't great, but I've never experienced more lag-based problems in one match than I have with Halo 4. Not sure why that is. Sometimes the countdown to the game starting will freeze and your screen will be blurred over and you can hardly see your gun for the first 3 minutes of the game. Sometimes the person you were just shooting at disappears and five seconds later it turns out you're dead. The gameplay itself is okay, so long as you aren't used to Halo 3 or Reach enough to get pissed off. They added customizable loadouts, which is a good thing to an extent, except for when that douche who is really good with plasma grenades and armor lock uses that for the entire game. The community of Halo 4 is pretty similar to that of Reach, lots of kids, the occasional racist texan. The maps are also from the campaign and Spartan Ops.
Forge has some tools like magnets and gravity volumes that make building maps a little easier, but it's too bad that none of the maps you can use in forge are anything like Forge World from Halo: Reach. They have something called "Forge Island" which you download separately and is supposed to be their substitute of Forge World. It may just be my opinion, but its kind of a piece of crap.
One last thing I wanna add is the styling. This game has appeared to have downgraded since Reach, making armor, enemies, landscapes, weapons, etc. look less realistic and more like a cartoon. The colors are ridiculously vivid (everything looks like the "Juicy" effect from Reach), much less armor detail, and seemingly mono-colored meshes.
Overall, I was not happy with this game. I understand that 343 is trying to keep Halo alive without a lot of Bungie's support, but they're not doing as good a job as they could be. This is all from my almost a year of experience playing this game, and I just feel like I should give my input on Halo 4. Hopefully Halo 5 will be better.
rows.title: Sad excuse of a Halo game

rows.text:
Catch-22, the renowned classic by Joseph Heller, tells the story of an outrageous Air Force squadron stationed on a Mediterranean island during World War II.

The story centers around Yossarian, possibly the only sane character in the book, and his struggle to not get killed. He is convinced that everyone is out to get him and given that he faces enemy fire on each bombing mission and that Colonel Cathcart continually raises the number of missions that must be flown in order to be discharged, he might be right.

Insanity, however, which runs deep in the squadron, offers a way out. If you’re crazy, all you have to do to get out of combat duty is ask. But there’s a catch - Catch-22 - which states that anyone asking to get out of combat duty is showing a concern for his safety which is the process of a rational mind and therefore proof that he is not crazy.

Other types of Catch-22’s abound in this irreverent tale of idiocy and chaos. Time is hazy and we feel as if we’re experiencing flashbacks as we jump back and forth between stories that unfold little by little until the details of a few jarring episodes are fully revealed at the end.

While the book provides plenty of comedy and ultimately ends with a surreal and frightening look at the horrors of humanity, two solid pluses, there were surprisingly few laugh-out-loud moments and a fair amount of corny and tiresome lines.

Heller is clearly a huge fan (and possibly a pioneer) of slap-you-in-the-face irony which was hilarious before I had pubes but is so pervasive and monotonous that it makes me wanna spit on babies. For instance, on the third page of the book we have:

"The Texan turned out to be good-natured, generous and likable. In three days no one could stand him."

It would have been nice if Heller had omitted many of these duds but as with everything in life there are exceptions. For example, after Chief White Halfoat recites his family’s long tale of hardship, he concludes with the following.

"Racial prejudice is a terrible thing, Yossarian. It really is. It’s a terrible thing to treat a decent, loyal Indian like a n***er, k*ke, w*p or sp*c."

And while I correctly & astutely complain about lame slap-you-in-the-face irony gags, what makes this book great (and it is great*) is the cast of characters – the delinquents, imbeciles, and butt monkeys – that populate the squadron.

In addition to Yossarian, this includes Dunbar – my personal hero and guiding light – who cultivates boredom to increase his lifespan; Orr, the grinning pygmy with crab apple cheeks; Aarfy – good old Aarfy; Nately, who’s madly in love with a prostitute who couldn’t be more bored with him; McWatt, who may be the craziest of all because he’s perfectly sane yet doesn’t mind war; and Major Sanderson, a staff psychiatrist with an acute interest in intense sex dreams.

To sum up, Catch-22 comprises key features that make a great book. It’s assinine, irreverent (Heller pokes fun at the military, the government, and humanity in general), and ultimately moving. And while I recommend this book to everyone – especially those of you studying for the SATs – its lack of laugh-out-loud moments and Heller’s occasionally corny sense of humor drop it down to a still extremely respectable 4 pearls.

Bonus quote: "Like Olympic medals and tennis trophies, all [the parade pennants] signified was that the owner had done something of no benefit to anyone more capably than anyone else."

*This book would be an absolute gem if every chapter was like Ch. 27 and if someone would chop about a third off the page count.
rows.title: An Amusingly Chaotic Tale of Idiots & Irony

rows.text:
Spike Lee's Bamboozled owes a lot to Paddy Chayefsky's story Network (1976), which owes a lot to writer Budd Schulberg's A Face in the Crowd (1957). All three movies are about TV personalities who become the personification of social or political movements.

In Bamboozled, Spike Lee makes his debt to Network explicit by having a black TV announcer tell his TV audience to go to their windows, open them, and shout to the world that they're "not going to take it any more," just as Peter Finch's Edward R. Murrow-type news anchor did in Network. Then the star of the TV show in Bamboozled collapses, like the news anchor.

In Bamboozled, the black dancer who stars in The New Millennium Minstrel Show loses his identity and becomes a stereotype. The network changes his name from Manray (an artist, like Man Ray?) to Mantan (after Mantan Moreland, the bulgy-eyed comic in old movies).

When Network first came out, everything it predicted was outrageous, but it's all reality (or reality TV) now. TV news departments are no longer subsidized by entertainment divisions - - news departments ARE entertainment divisions, or more precisely, news IS entertainment. Sibyl the Soothsayer in Network has become John Edward, crossing over between the spirit world and a country that finds superstition more comforting than science. (We're desperate not to be Left Behind.)

In an interview in Cineaste magazine (vol. XXVI, no. 2, 2001), Spike Lee tells why he dedicated Bamboozled to Budd Schulberg, the writer of A Face in the Crowd, the movie that made Andy Griffith a star.

Andy Griffith is like John Wayne. He only played one character on film and TV - - himself - - and Griffith's Lonesome Rhodes in A Face in the Crowd is like Wayne's Ethan Edwards in The Searchers. The cruelty in Ethan Edwards and Lonesome Rhodes is also there in every other character the two actors ever played, but we didn't want to see it. And after his success in A Face in the Crowd, Griffith never again dared showed the kind of contempt that Sheriff Andy Taylor must have had for the citizens of Mayberry. Giving us a glimpse of the monster is one thing, but Griffith knew America couldn't stand to see him every week. Exasperation with with Barney was as bad as it got.

So we got the phony Mayberry smile - - a kind of whiteface, another mask.

That issue of Cineaste is worth tracking down for articles on the reaction to Bamboozled and for Spike Lee's interview. Lee talks about what he calls the "magical n****r" in movies, "magical Negroes who appear out of nowhere" and use their powers "for the benefit of the white stars of the movies." Films like The Green Mile, The Family Man, and The Legend of Bagger Vance.

Now we're all tuned in to the Network and it's picked out A Face in the Crowd for us.

Everybody likes him when they meet him (God knows why, since he's such an obvious bully to people around him). He used to drink too much and got into some trouble, but he's turned his life around and, with the support of friends with money, he's become a national leader. The people who warn he's a fascist are just left-wingers "out of the mainstream" who hate America anyway.

Forget the Matrix, the United States has become Network. No definite article is needed to describe the all-embracing global corporate entity that is the military-industrial-entertainment complex. (Ned Beatty tries to explain this epiphany to Peter Finch at the end of the movie Network.)

And the United States is on its way to becoming the minstrel show Spike Lee shows in Bamboozled.

In Bamboozled, white people, Hispanics, and blacks all wear blackface to be in the audience and all answer yes to the question, "Is you a n****r?" Maybe the non-blacks are just "appropriating" a culture they have no right to, but it might be a good thing for people to realize they all have something in common.

Everybody's being bamboozled.
rows.title: "Is you is, or is you ain't? . . ."

rows.text:
1 - Intro: This is just a drum beat over several newscasters talking about what happened to Tupac so I usually skip over it.
2 - If I Die 2nite: My favorite song on the album, the rhymes really flow, the beat doesn't seem forced, and the even though the chorus is sort of minimalistic, it sounds really good. My only complaint is when 2pac says at the end kill kill kill, murda murda murda. This "growl" at the end doesn't keep the rest of the tone of the song. - 9/10
3 - Me Against the World: A very competent song, the beat is ok but its nothing special. The rhymes flow like always, and the chorus is ok. It's basically talking about how Tupac is against the world. It's not bad, but it's not great. - 7/10
4 - So Many Tears: Excellent song almost on the level of If I Die Tonight. Talking about whether Tupac would get into heaven and how he's so tired of "the game". I love the beat and the harmonica in the chorus. Because Ja Rule can't make any good beats on his own, the beat from this song was jacked by him, Nas, and Ashanti in the Pledge Remix. I don't know if it's in the original but it really makes me mad that they took an anti - gangsta beat and made it into a gangsta song. Pac was real, you are not Mr. Rule! - 8.5/10
5 - Temptations - This is a good song talking about how Pac is a player and he can't really settle down ever. Excellent chorus saying, "I know you've been searching for someone...". For some reason though, I find my self skipping over this song. I don't know why. - 7/10
6 - Young N***AZ - This a cool track with an instresting beat about a topic I haven't really heard any other rapper talk about before. I like the guys voice in the chorus. - 8/10
7 - Heavy in the Game - Ugh... one of the few bad songs. I hate the womans voice in the beginning. Sort of monotonus. - 4/10
8 - Lord Knows - Ehh... ok. The chorus gets old fast but its decent. - 6/10
9 - Dear Mama - Believe it or not, I actually didn't find this track too special. It was a little too sappy for me. But I guess I have to admit it's good, just not the best. - 8/10
10 - It Ain't Easy: Im sorry, noooo - 5/10
11 - Can U Get Away - Ok. - 8.5/10
12 - Old School - See #8
13 - F*** The World - Now this is nice. About his jail time and the courts whatever. Suprisingly upbeat beat but somehow it works. I like the rhymes and chorus. - 8.5/10
14 - Death Around the Corner - GAHH The weird Mexican guy in the chorus annoys the living heck out of me cause I can't figure out what he is saying. The beat is ok, I like the woman in the beginning and the rhymes is about Pac going slowly insane about his impending death. - 6/10 (Just for that guy in the Chorus.)
15 - Outlaw - The beginning is actually pretty sad to hear but the rest of the song is just a little better than average. - 7/10
Overall a classic album don't let me dissuade you from buying it just because of a couple bad songs.
If I Die 2Night, So Many Tears, F*** The World, and Young N***az are worth buying this album for.
rows.title: Great Album

rows.text:
When I saw that there was a male-version of 'Skinny Bitch' coming out, I was excited. I read 'Skinny Bitch' and, despite much of the criticism, found it to be one of those life-changing books. It convinced me that going vegetarian was the way to go and that trying to be vegan was also a worthwhile venture. By reading 'Skinny Bitch' and following some of the advice it contained, I managed to lose a good 40 or 50 pounds, with the help of exercise in addition to the nutrition tips.

Now, 'Skinny Bastard'. Here's my advice; if you're a guy and you were too embarrassed to be seen toting around and flipping through 'Skinny Bitch', this is your book. If you read 'Skinny Bitch', don't expect to get much more out of this book. Wisely, I read through some of the chapters and flipped through the book at a local bookstore and was astonished to find that someone had the audacity to pretty much take 'Skinny Bitch', pack in a few more pages, change the packaging, make it 'speak' more to men and call it a new book. This is a blatant rip-off of one book pretty much attempting to spawn a franchise.

In summary, good material, but read 'Skinny Bitch' or 'Skinny Bastard', not both. 'Skinny Bastard' is for men and looks like it may have a few additional tid-bits that its female equivalent doesn't. Otherwise, don't waste your time and money on a book you've probably already have read if you've read or own 'Skinny Bitch.'
rows.title: 'Skinny Bastard' nothing more than a re-packaged 'Skinny Bitchy'

rows.text:
Na na na na naaaa!

*Disclaimer: This post contains the story of chicken harvest… by said knife. 1. I live on a farm. 2. We raise animals for food (like eggs and milk and pork). 3. Ain’t no one got time for aggressive animals.

-Bought a “nice” 8 month old rooster from the hatchery I got my Australorps from the year before. Because I thought we could hatch some chicks. I named the rooster Chanticleer, for obvious reasons. He stayed in quarantine for two weeks away from my flock. Took him out and for two to three weeks, he was the low man on the totem pole. The girls picked on him a little and put him in his place. He was friends with our male duck. He SEEMED sweet! After that time, he became a real aggressive douche!

It started off with him being mean to the younger flock of chickens. Then he would bully the female ducks and his ex-best bud, our male duck. He was super rough with the hens he was mounting - like one had scabs and was going bald. Suddenly, he was getting vent on the regular and this cock turned into a real dick!

As time went on, he started getting aggressive with the kids and with me. I changed his name to Earl after the Chicks (previously known as the Dixie Chicks) song and found myself singing about his upcoming death often. You see, beautiful Earl was filled only with spite. He had pure evil running through his heart and soul. He ran on aggression and hate. He would run across the yard (we have about two acres) just to come and attack us. And this was not because he was “protecting his flock” because the girls would be off doing their own thing somewhere else. He didn’t even have a spur yet and just with his claws and beak alone he drew blood. It didn’t matter if you kicked him off of you, he kept coming and attacking, and attacking, and attacking! It was like he was rabid.

We tried the “hold the rooster and let him know who is boss” but as I value my eyeballs, this was not a viable solution, and anytime anyone was close enough to catch Earl, you weren’t, he was attacking you.

Once I was squatted down feeding all the hens and ducks yummy freeze dried meal worms as treats, he came up behind me (while I was petting our cat) and attacked me in the back. He was literally at the OPPOSITE end of the yard (2 acres!!) and came over just to attack me! I started walking around with a stick, or spraying him with the hose as he tried to attack me anytime I had to do chores like feeding or watering animals. Earl was RELENTLESS!!! Literally would RUN across an acre as soon as he spotted you JUST to attack.

I was bent over cleaning out the truck, the back of my legs were out of the vehicle and he came up from under the porch and got me real good! Pecked a hole in my leg and scratched me all to hell. I was wearing pants, mind you and still ended up with a puncture wound. Unfortunately, I was also wearing crocs (great farm shoes, poop washes right off!) and despite me kicking the absolute living heck out of his stupid rooster raged body, he just kept coming! I am convinced he couldn’t feel pain, but also, crocs aren’t the best shoes for crazed-rooster defense. Fortunately, I had a bit of wood floor transition in the truck as I was doing home renovations, and I grabbed it out and took a swing. Not fazed at all! However, once I started coming at him in an absolute fit of rooster-killing-rage, he Brave Sir Robin-ed it the eff away from me. At this point I’d already ordered this knife, and a slaughter cone and I was just waiting for their deliveries.

As to the knife, it’s sharp! Earl died swiftly, and despite the theory of wanting that bastard to suffer, the reality is I’m really not for it. He was dispatched and harvested as humanely as possible. Earl went from “I hate him” to “I ate him”. Goodbye Earl! As to the knife, it is excellent!
rows.title: (Great knife!) Because Earl had to die!

rows.text:
In 2002 Ja was getting criticized more than ever, not to mention 50 was blowing up with "Wanksta" and the fans ended up completely turning on him. I still bought the album at the time anyway. Ja had his back against he wall, G-Uint was dissin him every chance they got and took Ja out of his zone, but I'll get more into that when I review his "Blood In My Eye" album he dropped in 03.The first single was "Thug Lovin featuring Bobby Brown", followed by the Ashanti assisted "Memerized, which was one of the worst career moves he could of made, and the video for it was inspired by the movie "Grease". That was not as good as his previous radio friendly singles ("Down A** B****", "Always On Time").

Ja returns to his old self on "Pop N*****", "Murder Reigns" (feat. Celeste Scalone), "Destiny (Outro)", "The Warning" but he cant recapture the magic he once had, then on West Coast Collab "Connected" (feat. Eastwood & Crooked I) he disses 50 & Dmx with the following lines "I'm the avenging angle and earth be thy claim/ And Ja be thy name I know your all praying/ For the day of my diminishing/ Why don't somebody finish them off and put it right through his cross/ The X's The 50'S ya'll gotta be kidding me These n***** is my sons/ Raised them from young/ Curtis and lil Earl/ should of been little girls/ Coz they b****made now they act like one of my itchbays Touche !", and "Last Temptation" (feat. Charli Baltimore) has a nice throwback vibe to it. Overall this album was all over the place, Rule sounds uninspired on "Emerica" (feat. Chink Santana & Young Life) & "Rock Star", and the skits after a few songs were really pointless. This is nowhere near his best work, Ja is talented but this album is barely 3 stars.
rows.title: The Last Temptation , Not His Best Work

rows.text:
I think that NaS is as strong as a MC as he ever was and I think that lyrically and idealistically, this album would be a 5 star. The only problem(s) that brings NaS down is the beats. I have always thought that you didn't need good beats to make a classic album,that you just needed the rhymes and skills on the mic,but boy was I wrong. The beats on this CD are just OK for NaS. I HATE the beat for "Mastermind" I think its too early 90s RNB sounding and his flow didn't go top the beat. I wasn;t feeling it at all. Other beats that I didn't like was the ALicia Keys produced "Warrior Song". It was just a off beat piano that sped up and was only on beat for the drum breakdown.Beats aside, NaS comes through lyrically as expected. His story telling skills on "Get Down" shine through gorgeously, while his street anthem "Made You Look" is something that you could just chill out to and nod ya head at the same time. NaS covers ideas such as spirituality on "Heaven" and tells all in his name dropping auto bio "Last Real Nigga Alive". His inspirational track "I Can" is OK,I suppose and "Zone Out"(feat Bravehearts) is just average,too. Jungle and Wiz don't prove to be anything above average for a commercial MC,while NaS completely KILLS the track(it has a sick beat too).Overall This CD is pretty good and is worth a listen if you're into supporting real rap,or if you're a NaS fan in general.

Top 5 tracks

1.Get Down

2.Heaven

3.Made You Look

4.Last Real Nigga Alive

5.I Can or Zone Out
rows.title: Classic Nas Album for 2003 Hip-hop

rows.text:
During the golden age of films, prior to television, massive, palatial, ornately decorated theaters were constructed as going to the movies was often an event. After television came about, many of these theaters (which subsequently suffered from neglect as audiences dwindled), particularly those in the inner cities, began catering to different audiences, becoming what was known as grindhouse theaters, with those located on New York's 42nd Street probably being the most relatable examples. Grindhouses would specialize in showing the stuff you probably wouldn't take your mother to see, as the films were often extremely graphic, sleazy, contemptible, and schlocky in nature from both foreign and domestic distributors...blaxploitaion, sexploitation (hard and soft core), hixploitation, nunsploitation, violent chop socky features, Japanese monster films, European horror sleaze, gore, giallo (Italian thrillers), `mondo' style films, nudie cuties...these were many of the various genres popular prior to the subsequent disappearance of the grindhouse venues by the mid to late 1980s, as the areas these theaters were located in were populated by what was generally considered the underbelly of society, and became easy targets as city leaders sought to garner favor with their constituents by sweeping clean the `scum', so to speak...but I digress...

In this DVD release of 42nd Street Forever Volume 1, Synapse Films provides an excellent representation of the diversity of the material popular over the years in these palaces of sleaze, in the form of 47 trailers, which were often better, by the way, than the actual features. Here's a listing of the trailers you get for the price of admission;

The Undertaker and His Pals (1966)

The Flesh and Blood Show (1972)

Women and Bloody Terror (1969)/Night of Bloody Horror (1969)

Blood Spatter Bride (1972)/I Dismember Mama (1974)

Corruption (1967)

The Butcher of Binbrook aka Graveyard of Horror (1971)

Italian Stallone (1970), a porno flick featuring a young Sylvester Stallone!

There's just over two hours of material on this DVD, presented in 1.78:1 letterboxed, enhanced for 16X9 TVs, with obviously formatted from different aspect ratios. The Audio is Dolby Digital 2.0 mono. Both the picture and audio quality vary from trailer to trailer, but most are quite good.

rows.text:
Free State of Jones is the story of Newt Knight, a poor subsistence farmer in Jones County Mississippi, a land ruled by the whims of large plantation owners. He would briefly serve for the Confederacy before going AWOL and leading a band of Anti-Confederate fighters in their resistance against the newly formed government. Director Gary Ross’s film, Free State of Jones tells the story of New Knight as well as Davis Knight, Newt's ancestor who, although he appeared white by today's standards, was brought up on miscegenation charges for marrying a white women because in Mississppi at the time, if you had one-eighth African blood, then you were considered legally black. Free State of Jones is an important film that stretches our understanding of both the Civil War and its aftermath, Reconstruction. Ross seems intent on bending the traditional Civil War narrative in order to get the viewer to see how the problem of race in America was not settled with the close of the war in 1865.

Newt Knight worked as a medic during the war, and when we first see him, he’s exchanging a wounded soldier’s garb for an officer’s jacket so that the man will be treated more quickly. Already disillusioned with the war, Knight becomes incensed when he discovers the implementation of the Twenty Negro Law, which exempted those who owned twenty slaves or more from military service, making his service part of, as Knight puts it, “a rich man's war and a poor man's fight.”

Knight abandons the army and returns home to his wife and child where he finds that the Confederacy has been seizing a disproportionate number of food and goods for the war effort. After turning guns on some Confederate soldiers to prevent them from taking any more, he flees to the swamps and lives alongside a number of slave maroons.

Knight strikes up a friendship with an escaped slave Moses as well as Rachel, a woman still tied to her plantation, but who manages to smuggle supplies to the escaped slaves. The film treats this as an experience of cross-racial empathy. Not only does Knight live with these escaped slaves, but he also depends on them. Unlike Knight, these men and women are knowledgeable about the swamp, able to navigate its inscrutable waters and wrest food from its harsh corners.

After the battle of Vicksburg, which essentially cleaved the Confederacy in half, more deserters trickle into Jones County, and Knight begins to rally a resistance, a resistance that includes both black and whites. Using guerilla tactics, Knight and his men eventually wrest Jones County and the surrounding area away from the Confederacy. During this time, he also strikes up a romance with Rachel. (At this point in time, Knight’s wife has fled the county, afraid that her husband’s lawlessness will mean repercussions for her and her child.)

Ross’s film is attempting to reinsert the notion of class into the Civil War narrative. Knight’s journey takes him from a realization that as a poor farmer, he’s being used as fodder to prop up an unjust economic system to the understanding that he has much in common with enslaved blacks of the South. This is not to mean that the plight of the poor white man is just as bad as that of enslaved persons. It certainly wasn’t. And slave narratives at the time from people like Frederick Douglass and Harriet Jacobs took pains to make it clear that slavery was a far worse situation than poverty. Still, both Knight and the escaped slaves he’s taken up with are being chewed up by an unjust economic system.

In a pivotal scene, Moses is chastised for taking food that one of Knight’s men believes belongs only to the other whites, not to “n***ers.” Moses responds, “How you ain’t a n***er?” This of course can be read a number of ways. This poor white has also been used by Confederacy, conscripted into a war that does not benefit him. It could also be read as a comment on the illusion of race as a biological category, an absurdity that becomes clear in the film through the intermittent intrusion of Davis Knight’s story.

In a film of this nature, it would make narrative sense to end at the close of the Civil War, perhaps also covering the fact that Knight and Rachel had to flee their home for fear of repercussions, just so that the ending isn’t too “Hollywood.” But nearly the last half hour of the film covers the Civil War’s aftermath and Reconstruction. Moses’s son is taken by a plantation owner as an “apprentice,” a form of slavery that persisted after the Civil War. We also witness the political disenfranchisement and violence blacks faced at the hands of terrorist organizations like the Ku Klux Klan.

By extending the narrative, Ross points out the long-term destructive force unleashed by slavery, which was not stopped at Appomattox, at the signing of the Emancipation Proximation, or at the passage of the 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments. Like Knight’s descendents, we too are living in the shadow of slavery and the Civil War.

As a filmmaker, Ross is often faulted for being too much of a crowd pleaser, and you can see his desire to make buoyant entertainment in his screenplays for Big and Dave or in his directorial efforts like Seabiscuit. There’s really nothing inherently wrong with this instinct for making an audience happy, but I think critics also forget that Ross also directed the smart and nuanced take on Plato’s “Allegory of the Cave,” Pleasantville. And there’s a real desire on his part in push the Civil War drama forward in the hopes of counteracting the Lost Cause narrative that has infected the public imagination. And in doing so, he plays with the structure of Free State of Jones in unique and interesting ways.

That’s not to say that Free State of Jones is a perfect film. I can’t quite decide if it’s too short or too long. Davis Knight’s story seems essential to what Ross is attempting to accomplish with this film, but his intrusion into the nineteenth-century narrative is sometimes clunky. And while Ross worked with historians to make sure the details of his film were right, by necessity some of history’s weirdness gets sanded down. For instance, by the end of the film, Knight’s first wife comes back to live with him and Rachel, but we don’t really get a sense of what she would have thought about her husband taking up with another woman, especially a former slave. (Although, it is suggested in the film that Knight may have been carrying on a sexual relationship with both women, which the historical record appears to back up).

But Free State of Jones is a timely and necessary film. We live in an age where racism has forced many poor and working class whites to vote against their own interests. And it’s worth remembering that this has historical precedent that goes back generations. In time, I expect the reputation of Free State of Jones to increase. It’s another important film in a string of them that asks us to reexamine nineteenth-century America in order for us to understand how we’ve gotten into the mess we’re currently in.
rows.title: An Underrated Gem

rows.text:
This album is just as excellent as Dr. Dre's first album The chronic. Once again his beats and rapping is awesome. I will explain what my opinion of each track one by one and tell you How many stars I'd Give each one out of five.
1. Lolo Intro - The introduction to the album. A skit at that skippable because I normally don't like skits.
2. The Watcher - This is an excellent opening song for the album that really gives you a great idea of what the tone of the album is. It has a great beat, intersting lyrics, a cool chours and the usual great rapping from Dre. 5 stars.
3. **** You - I am not really that fond of this track. I do like the beat a lot but I hate the way the chours is sung and this song has very uninteresting lyrics. The good beat cannot save this track. 2 stars
4. Still D.R.E. - An excellent track where Dr. Dre and Snoop Dogg each come at full force and spit interesting lyrics about how they are the same way they were back in the early 1990s over an excellent beat to fit with all the rapping perfectly. 5 stars.
5. Big Egos - Another excellent track with a great beat, great rapping and a beautifully sung chours. 5 stars.
6. XXplosive - Another excellent track with a great guitar riff for the beat that makes it have a cool Rock n Roll sound to it. It also has great rapping and some beatiful tuneful singing by Nate Dogg. 5 Stars.
7. What's The Difference - Another excellent track with an interesting unique horn laced beat, Great rapping by Dr. Dre, Xzibit and Eminem, and interesting lyrics where Dr. Dre details his feelings towards his former N.W.A. Bandmates. 5 stars.
8. Bar One - A skit so I don't bother to give it a rating.
9. Light Speed - A cool track with a cool beat, great rapping and a well done chours that really sounds cool. 4.5 stars.
10. Forgot About Dre - An Excellent track with a fun bouncy beat where Dr. Dre speaks of how he deserves respect for all the contributions he's made to the rap music world. An Eminem being featured on it makes it even better. 5 stars.
11. The Next Episode - Another great track with just what I always expect from Dre a great beat, great rapping and a catchy chours. 5 stars.
12. Let's get High - a pretty good track with a cool beat but the rapping is not quite as good as in the other songs on the album but it's still good. 3.5 stars.
13. B***h N***az - A not so great track. the beat is not good like his other songs and the rapping is not good as usual. 2 stars.
14. The Car Bomb - A dramatic skit about a car bomb explosion in a car. I like this skit better than the other ones because of it's dramatic content.
15. Murder Ink. An Ok track but not one of my favorites. The beat is not quite as good as most of the beats on this album but it's still pretty good and Dre is not here to make the track excellent 3.5 stars.
16. Education - a plain old skippable skit.
17. Some L.A N***as - An ok track but not one of my faves on the album. 3 stars.
18. Pause 4 **** Skippable skit.
19. Housewife - A great track with great rapping and beats.5 stars.
20. Ackrite. A cool track with a cool bassline for the beat. 4 stars.
21. Bang Bang A great track with cool rapping, a great beat and intersting sound effects for the chours. 4.5 stars.
22. The Message - A great track honoring his late brother Tyree with a great mellow harp for the beat. 5 stars.
rows.title: Once again Dr. Dre did it for me.

rows.text: Juvenile deserves the title he used on his last album. He's been going strong since the early 90's and it was only a matter of time before a Greatest Hits album came out. All of the songs on here are his hits and everyone can find something they like on this. The best song on here (one of my all-time favorite rap songs) is "Back That Azz Up", which has a unique delivery and a tight beat to back them up. The delivery heard in "Ha" actually made me laugh for some reason, because it's so strange and it's almost like he's talking. "In My Life" is another hit, because it's gangsta and the beat is tight. "Bounce Back" is pretty good, and Baby AKA #1 Stunna provides his thoughts on it. "Rich N***az" is tight, because it has several members of the Hot Boyz and the beat is hard. Every song on here is produced by Mannie Fresh except for "Slow Motion", which I don't like as much because of its overplayed status on the radio. But other than that, this album is good and listenable. A couple songs are skippable ("Mamma Got Azz" and a couple others) but I just don't listen to 'em. If you're a Juvenile fan and don't have this yet, go buy it, you idiot!!!
rows.title: Tha Great

rows.text:
Im a sucker for mysterious evil creature/entity stories. I love Alien, the Thing, etc. so naturally I was drawn to the story of this book. If you like a little story with a huge heaping of F**k phrases, then this book is for you. I agree with other posters as far as redundancies and plot holes, and underdeveloped characters, so I want to offer Bryan Alaspa some advice. There are so many stories already written, I know its hard to find an original idea, so you need to recast that idea in an original way. I know you tried in this book with the "sound of hell recording" premise, but you got lost. For example, the CEO was too stereotypical...and was not in context with the today's corporate cultural climate...He was so 1980's.

Bryan, buy and read "The Anatomy of a story" by John Truby
http://www.amazon.com/Anatomy-Story-Becoming-Master-Storyteller-ebook/dp/B0052Z3M8A/ref=sr_1_1?s=digital-text&ie=UTF8&qid=1408018177&sr=1-1&keywords=anatomy+of+a+story

It will help.

Keep on writing, and for goodness sakes lay off the inordinate amount of F**ing phrases!! I am intelligent, mature reader and not in Jr. High School!!

F**g Rig
F**g thing
WTF
F**k out of here
F**g Last Name
F**g storm
F**g Demons
F**g this ...F**g that....
rows.title: Should have been called "F**ing Rig"
