rows.text:
Let me tell you a story. I tried Monster iSport Immersion with control talk, Klipsch Image S4i, Sony XBA-4IP and Boss MIE2i.

Each of these midrange earphones, and Sony XBA-4IP as of high range are totally different in their sound quality, comfort, quality and feel in various ways.
I tried Monster iSport, which seals sound in a better way than rest of others, but sounds mix of everything emphasizing bass. Then I tried Klipsch Image S4i, which is comparatively a huge improvement on comfort and feel along with considerably admirable bass and effective vocal clarity.

But, it didn't satisfy me because I was in search of a better earphones that I can have all what I would expect from. Then I went to buy Boss and then Sony. All piece of shit. Nothing that is worth to glorify: hey, I've these earphones! You will lose every penny you spent on them and never be able to compensate with these all brands I mentioned unless one maker comes ahead and delivers what we really want.

In my totally personal experience:

1. Earphones suck because they hurt and annoy you instead of pleasing you for extended period of use. Must of us hate this because we want to enjoy music. And most of the brands just suck never being able to realize what comfort means to an extended time music listener. So we desperately want a kind of earphones that at least be able to please you not being painful for extended listening. If so, we love to have that earphones. Unfortunately, there is not a single brand that is so much comfortable, aesthetically pleasing, able to provide decent ambient sound elimination, cable quality and durability, appealing driver housing, great bass and vocal clarity, simplified control talk, solid L-shape 3.5mm jack housing and cute divider.

2. The look, feel and material of earphones just suck. Most of the midrange and high-range earphones have been using disgusted material that neither looks pleasing, nor feels awesome. We hate most of the look and feel of the earphones material. Some even smell shit. Some smell morphine and other smell smoke or kind of disgusting thing we never like. My daughter throws when I open the package of some earphones that spread motion sickness right away. Moreover, the bending, delicate wiring, fragile housing, spiteful look and terrible engineering piss me off when I pick up most of the earphones brands.

3. What drives me crazy about most of the earphones including Boss, Sony, Monster, Klipsch, Shure, Ultimate Ears, Synthesizer and you name, is their control talk design. I like to throw a hammer over the engineers that designed the control talk button in most of the low to mid to high range earphones. The technology has taken a leap and their workaround is still based on century back concept of architecture and design. None of the makers has any technology and engineering visionary that can apply an art in the control talk or the divider angle of the earphones. When I touch most of the earphones' control talk button, I like to curse at their stupid brain that even can't think an inch better for design, feel, navigation, and access and aesthetic beauty of the product. The same thing applies for the cable, coil, housing and jack. They all look and feel horrible.

4. In respect to sound clarity, distinct vocals, appealing melody, pleasing treble, magnetizing bass and infused guitar and electronics, most of the earphones suck, just suck.

5. I don't believe why technology, art, engineering, beauty, comfort and durability can't go together. These sucky earphones are looting our 100s of dollars and offering just shit. Something like this: Boos has not great bot good feel of cable though but lacks sound isolation horribly, torchers the engineering of earpiece and raises the house par your ear, abuses the art of L shape jack housing mercilessly and dumps architectural appeal to the ditch.

6. Look into Klipsch that keep designing most of the earphones look like a dick abusing genitals into the ears and embarrassing its users and admirers who have stories how quickly they pack up the earphones back into their pockets every single use. The same applies while using them hiding between fingers and quickly inserting into the ears so that nobody notices what shape is getting pushed into the ears. Kind of naked to shamelessness.

7. When we use Monster, seems like they absolutely don't care about piece of engineering and architecture for fragile cable and rough ear buds that is why they just throw thoughtless cables, and cheap buds that feels like you are pushing wooden craps into your ear canal in about 10 minutes of use. I have no idea whether they least learn about the physics and anatomy of ear canal and its sensitivity, resistance and bearing capacity. Seems like most of the earpiece makers think for tormenting eardrum. They want to beat our eardrums and beat shit out of them as much as possible; it's harassing in nature!

8. The suckiest part of the earphone makers is multi-driver unhealthy competition. I recently happened to purchase Sony XBA-4IP. Was expecting greater quality due to hyped 4 drivers claiming balanced amateur delivery and noise attenuation, comfort and clarity. What best I found in this piece of shit is only the carrying case, which is cute but not innovative.

9. Not only Sony, seems like almost all makers have lost their taste in innovation that is inherited with the best practiced engineering, craftsmanship, art, science & technology, comfort, simplicity and design in one simple package. One brand gives good cable but loses the rest. The other brand gives bass and the rests will come as pieces of whole stinking shit. Some master in vocals and not a damn thought about artistic beauty and sensitivity of ear.

10. I'm sick of these makers: none of them make a beautiful piece of earphones that is elegant in use, comfortable to wear, loving in touch, brilliant in look, strong in construction, light in weight, artistic in design and that could appeal you to get one and listen songs of your favorite selections for hours and hours very comfortably, even not knowing your ears have something into them.

So what could make the best earphone pieces that would be pure and appealing? I believe that if the following ideas are mixed in one simple package, you goanna have the best earphones ever:

1. Give me the control talk size as exactly as of standard iPhone 4S earphone piece and the length of the talk button between mouth and chin. Any length par will have direct impact to call quality, noise cancellation and wind isolation.

2. Give me the earpieces with the comfort of Klipsch but improving it to completely isolate ambient surrounding sound (and omitting the dick-like shape at the housing, just a suggestion to Klpsch).

3. Give me the driver housing like Sennheiser IE80 but modifying it with the omission of sound adjustment slot and converting it into the curved-angle triangular shape.

4. Give me the cable just like SkullCandy offers tangle free cable even to its cheapest pieces but offering elegantly bright, shiny and soothing color that is totally contrast to the existing color and would stand alone and giving the feel of lust of cable somehow like of Boss MIE2i but mastering it to the next level.

5. Give me 3.5mm jack housing quality like of Monster iBeats but modifying it into L-shape with the same elegance, brilliance, size, material and finish. Believe or not, it feels like jewel when you hide the cable and 3.5mm jack only to expose the iBeats jack housing.

6. Give me the bass improving over Boss MIE2(i) and just above iBeats but soothing like Klipsch does in most earpieces.

7. Give me the clarity like Sony XBA-4 but improving it not to dominate bass neither struggling separation.

8. Give me the case like Sony XBA-4IP but with belt mount or built-in pocket clip improving it to offering an elegant exterior slot that circles round the case mounting the cable and residing the jack and earpiece parts only inside the case, i.e. exposing the cable. The case flap would flip open and close with magnetizer. Obviously genuine leather. Not larger than Sony's contemporary case. Have a look into it if you happened to find one somewhere. There's not another earphone case coming close to Sony's case offered with XBA-4IP.

9. Give me the cable replacement option offered such as Shure does but improving the detachable cable with Monster iBeats 3.5mm jack housing like strength offering next level of secure Snap-On magnetized edges that eliminates visibility of attachment line or ring and saves your cable on accidental knock. This part would be constructed with superior engineering craftsmanship and architectural engineering.

10. Give me the earpiece in such a package that even an 8-year-old kid becomes happy to open it from the box. Such simple packaging with a decent box just like the package of an iPhone 4S: tear the thin plastic membrane and open the box as if it is designed for you exclusively to have extensive flavor of opening the package making you feel special and offering to believe that the thing inside is so much better if the housing itself it that alluring. Most of the earphones packages just suck, frustrate and even make the feel of having the purchase worse. At times I was destroying the whole case and earphones when Boss and Klipsch shipped. These guys have designed the package as if somebody would rob them tearing the case just in front of them and so had to resist. If I were the CEO of either of the companies, I would fire the designers of the case right away or destroy their concept taking shit out of them over their design briefing meetings.

If I am offered an earphone package with the mentioned 10 commandment in earphone Design, Art, Engineering, Architecture, Science and Craftsmanship brought together under one visionary principal, I would say: Yes, that is it! We all would love to hear music for hours again. And it would make the dent in the Universe. And you will rule if you are able to make such.

Remember, this review is only based on the earphones I purchased for my own use. Nobody sent me any piece for review neither they are for free. I spent almost thousand dollars in buying these pieces of shit, some more, which are not even worth mentioning here. And none of them please me. I just hate them for many reasons and some I listed here for reference in wanting one that I would love to.

Amazon asked me to write review about Boss MIE2i, but what should I write since I only hate any that I can have access too. Nothing is any better. Every earphone I have used since 2005 suck from this or that reason. Each maker seems focusing only at one area missing the whole package we are obliged to pay for.

When a consumer buys a TV, s/he doesn't only pay for Screen size, but also pays for the design, casing, corners, stand, base line, side line, resolution, contrast, clarity, features, technology, sound, remote, and so much more as a whole package. When all come in an elegant way to my observation, we say: Yes, this is it. Same thing applies for earphones.

What I have found about earphones is, the makers are throwing a lot of talk about the capacity and deliverability of the drivers they have used and talking about how comfortable they are or how much noise they can cancel. That's bullshit. Give us a package that sounds great, looks, feels, lasts, shines, and exists with us great. Talk about cable quality and lavishness on it. Talk about talk button exclusively. Talk about housing and its fit as if on debate. Talk about bass realistically. Talk about vocal and clarity distinctively. Talk about materials you have used in shaping 3.5mm jack and housing. Talk about invisible wire lines you have used and their capacity in delivering lossless sound waves into the drivers. Talk about since, engineering and art involved. Talk about the case that is specifically designed for the product for specific reasons and benefits to the user. Boss throws a case that fits most of the phones including iPhone - what an unwise thought! Klipsch throws a case made of tin like cheap material that doesn't only look cheap, feels extremely humiliating when you observe it - a brand of chewing gum ships in the same kind of box and a brand of chewing tobacco from India is infamous shipping on such case. I would like to spit on it and flush into toilet as many times I receive them.

If you are a music lover and care aesthetic beauty and art in the material you use, earphone makers have a greater challenge to accomplish which none of them will be sure daring to set forth. The only reason is that none of them has a visionary fellow that could render ideas into a beautiful and useful simple package we all are thriving to have for years. Until then, though I hate Boss MIE2i, I will be using it replacing with Sony or Klipsch or iSport occasionally.
rows.title: All Earphones Suck

rows.text:
The first book Diamonds is ridiculous and Delilah is naive to the point of idiocy. She is nice to a fairy and in turn is given the gift to make huge sums of money. This is handy, because of a crime her father committed (that is not revealed) Delilah and her family went from being super rich to broke and socially disgraced. However this is clearly the rich version of broke because Delilah still can afford to hang out with the same social set, and does not seem to have a job other than throwing charity fundraisers with her friends. One of these friends is Chas, a hot, rich, businessman. What is his business? No idea. He is a womanizer and serial Fiancé. Soon after discovering her windfall Delilah runs to tell her friend Chas so that he can help her invest, seeing as he's a "businessman" and all. Chas however is in dire financial straits. So upon learning of her new financial status instead of asking his friend for a loan, he whips out the returned engagement ring from his latest ex-Fiancé and asks her to marry him. But he's not a bad guy because his plan is not to actually marry her. He's just going to temporarily take over her finances, take her money and invest it in his company, then pay her back with interest. All without her knowledge of course. And even though they have not had even a remotely romantic relationship Delilah says yes to his proposal and the shenanigans begin.

I have so many problems with this plot. Chas is a bad guy, he is using Delilah and he never does anything to redeem himself. His big apology is laughably lame. Also unbeknownst to Delilah, Chas has also slept with her sister and Toad heroine Isadora. Chas is the poster boy for a rich douche bag. I understand that Delilah lacks self esteem, but her lack of common sense was annoying. I also didn't understand if Delilah was overweight or curvy. She was called plus size in one sentence, then her size was compared to Bettie Page and Marilyn Monroe. Neither of those women were overweight. So was Delilah just annoyed by those extra 10/15/20 pounds keeping her from a model size 2. Or is she a size 14/16/18 wishing she could be thinner. These are two different psychological issues. Delilah is also both the Madonna and Whore trope wrapped up in one neat little package. The ending is supposed to be a happy one, but the way Chas behaves I really don't see how there could be a ever after.

The second story Toads is no better. Isadora is a spoiled, gold digging, lying, slut. No I don't mean that in a "slut shaming" way, I mean it literally. She is a cheater, she has no respect for her own relationships or others, and she uses sex as a weapon to get what she wants. She knows the fairy is out granting blessings but can't be nice for five minutes so ends up getting cursed. Her curse is that everything that comes out of her mouth is sexual. Sam is Isadora's ex-Fiancé, and Chas' ex-bestfriend. Why? Because Isadora cheated on Sam with Chas and then continued sleeping with him after the break up. So Sam is shocked when he get's an invitation to Chas' engagement party, thinking he would be marrying Isadora. Instead he is marrying her sister Delilah and Chas asked Sam to be his best man. Isadora is cut off by her mother, and needs Sam's help to survive. Cut off from what I don't know because Eudora refers to them as penniless, and again our heroine is unemployed.

Once again this story requires us to suspend belief and not because of the magic. It's been 8 years since the break up. Sam has moved on with his life and doesn't live in Texas, why would he come back to celebrate his philandering ex-friend. Also Sam and Chas never hash it out. Chas doesn't ask for forgiveness, he says "you're over that now, right". And when Sam makes a comment about the fact that Isadora cheated on him with Chas, Chas get's in his face about it. Sam takes it, and apologizes to Isadora for making a "rude" comment. Are you freaking kidding me. I don't even want to get started on how Sam has spent the last 8 years with his relationships, or should I say lack thereof.

I have no idea why the blurb says that Delilah and Isadora combined would make the perfect woman, they are both idiots. Combining the two would be disastrous. Chas is a complete douche and is one of the worst hero's of any book I've ever read. Sam is a decent guy, but seriously screwed up and needs intense therapy. All of these characters are in their 30's but they behave like teenagers. The only reason why I did not give this book in one star is that it's a fast read. This is two short stories combined to make a short book that is written more like fan fiction than a novel.
rows.title: Suspense, of belief...

rows.text:
OMG!!! Where do I start? I should start out by saying I REALLY, REALLY HATE this book. I've read a couple of this author's novels over the years, they were okay, but this one takes the cake for being a vile, piece of garbage that should've been sent to the reject pile. Written around 1980, I guess the bodice rippers were making their way out of the disco era where it was common for women to be marginalized, seen as sexual objects, and used and abused in every romance novel by Rosemary Rogers and the like. I have a feeling Ms. Robards was trying to emulate the former. Anyone remember Sweet Savage Love? If you haven't. Then good for you. I literally burned my copy years ago it was so awful. But I digress.

This clinker has a thirty-something man going through what I guess you could call a middle age crisis. Somewhere along the line, Justin's misspent youth was snatched from him when he took on the guardianship of a young girl, Megan when she was five. I suppose his later abuse of her is rooted in deep hostility. Or it could be because he's a sociopath who preys on seventeen year-old girls fresh out of boarding school. That's where dear little Megan has spent most of her life. They hardly know each other. He pays the bills, provides room and board, and doesn't have to deal with her at all until she comes home ready and ripe for the plucking. This entire story is horrifically disturbing. From Justin's indifference in the beginning, to the fatherly outrage, to the slow realization that he's physically attracted to her despite the fact that he's been a father figure to her for years, suddenly makes you realize you're reading a story about child abuse.

Because that's what Megan is, a seventeen year-old virgin with no inkling about the world or men. Suddenly she is infatuated with her protector. But Justin is far from that. From the moment he starts seeing her as a sexual object rather than an innocent child in need of his protection, the book goes downhill fast. Instead of trying to distance himself from her maybe by trying to find her a suitable young man to marry, Justin's lust clouds whatever judgment he had. Believe me, he hasn't much. He does everything he can to find ways to compromise her, to earn her trust, to tempt her into falling for his lies. He's basically a predator. And it's all an avalanche from there. Once he kisses her, that's it. In that day and age, she's done. So he takes it upon himself to finish the job. Of course poor Megan is often confused by her feelings for Justin. Poor thing hasn't the sense God gave a doorstop.

I think what made me the most angry was the scene the night after the deflowering. Megan is happy with herself and her newfound womanly knowledge. But of course she's wearing her white schoolgirl dress while basking in the glow of shame. Poor girl thinks Justin will marry her. No. He will not. He's already married! Gotcha! And our romantic pedophile is transformed into a raging, hormonal lunatic who is suddenly enraged with everything that Megan does or says. So what if he has a frigid wife? Megan should have known. So what if he gets her pregnant? Megan should have known. So what if he's an angry, unreasonable sack-of-you-know-what? Megan should have known better. And on and on. Everything that Justin does from here on out is all Megan's fault. He will justify every hateful word, every pinch, every bruise, and smack across the face as being necessary because she drove him to it. Sound familiar?

Well, by the time Megan decides Justin is a basket case and wants to provide for the child by finding a nice man to marry, Justin goes full retard and kidnaps her. Imprisons her. Marries her. And I'm getting tired. Yeah, he does a lot more. He also forces himself on her while denouncing her as a slut, whore, bitch, etc. He hits her so hard she trips, falls nearly over a cliff and loses the baby. It's all rather graphic. He's sooo sorry afterward. Yeah, we know. Justin is always sorry. Megan has no self-respect, will never find self-respect, and takes him back. He gets her pregnant again somehow, and they live happily ever after.

Ugh. There are better stories out there. Save yourself the mental anguish of reading this tripe. Excuse me while I bleach my eyes.
rows.title: Run, run as fast you can...from this shocking disgrace of a book!

rows.text:
This is really a great disc. The GZA isn't my favorite Wu-Tang MC and (people will hate my for this) I don't think he's the most talented (I'm gonna side with Ghost) and I certainly don't think this is the best We solo disc (Cuban Linx has a tighter concept.) But, again, this is a really great disc.

It showcases what the GZA does best, and that's old school rhymes with clever metaphors and punchlines. RZA's production on Liquid Swords is slowed down from Cuban Linx and even 36 Chambers, which allows the GZA to move at his own pace. The most prominent guests - Method Man and Inspectah Deck - work well to complement what is going on. Indeed, I don't think Deck has sounded better than this album (Duel of the Iron Mic, Cold World.) Meth's work on Shadowboxin' makes some of the dopest verses you'll ever hear ("fiend to catch a n***a snoozin'/slip the cardiac/arrest me/exorcist hip-hop possess me/crunch a n***a like a Nestle/you know my steeze")

The Genius also has a chance to shine on concept songs like Labels and Killah Hills 10304 in a way that he doesn't on Wu-Tang posse tracks. As mentioned by other reviewers, Labels is constructed around the names record labels and is a cautionary tale about thinking that a record deal is going to solve your problems ("Tommy ain't my muthaf***in Boy/when he fakes moves on a n***a he employ".) Killah Hills is the kind of in-depth drug dealer narrative that you would expect from Ghostface or Raewkwon, but the GZA holds his own, creating an even grimier scene than either of them (except maybe Ghost's "Malcom" from Supreme Clientele.)

The only "problems" occur when GZA flows over quicker beats with quicker MCs - notably on 4th Chamber and Investigative Reports. RZA, Rae and Ghost have such quick, dexterous flows and rapid fire rhymes that the songs seem to drag under the GZA's straight-forward, laid-back delivery. I realize this has more to do with preferences, so I still give the album five stars, but it's something that's always stood out to me.

All in all, this is a fantastic piece of work from a very talented MC and worth every penny you would ever have to pay for it. It makes me long for the golden era of mid-90's NY hip-hop. Buy, listen, enjoy.
rows.title: You know my steeze...

rows.text:
Dark Souls, as you've probably read from the other 323 reviews here, is a dark fantasy RPG legendary for its difficulty, obtuse-ness and mostly excellent combat system. When tasked to explain this game to one of my buddies recently, this is exactly what I said: "It's an awesome game, but you're going to die about 500 times at least. But you should still give it a try!" Not surprisingly, he hasn't tried it yet haha.

When I first started playing this game, there was an immediate sense of bewilderment and awe (especially when you first hit Firelink Shrine - the hub area - with its unique violin music and etc). There was an armored guy sitting down near a bonfire. I tried talking to him but he seemed at the time to mostly ramble on about strange things and laugh at me. (Actually, a TON of NPCs in this game like to laugh when you talk to them for some weird reason). He spoke of bells to ring, so me with my crap starting armor and heater shield walked down to what looked like the next area I should go to- a nice little cemetary just a staircase away from the bonfire.

Walking down those stairs, I was soon beset upon by some skeletons. WOW. All I can say is that unlike maybe every other RPG in existance, Dark Souls' skeletons are NOT the pushovers you'd expect. They move incredibly quickly, slicing and dicing you to bits causing heavy bleeding.. and then "YOU DIED" pops up. Get used to seeing this, by the way. I use this small skeleton story to illustrate an important aspect of the game though: Dark Souls is a game where everything you think you know about games like this is wrong. Hell, you can't even sell your excess items until at least halfway through the thing!

Some things are unfair, and I'm not just talking about combat-affiliated gripes. I grew up on the NES. I'm used to games that don't tell you where to go or what to do and all that, but Dark Souls has a couple instances where I just don't see how anybody could progress through it without a guide of some sort. Case in point: I found a city of the dead filled with ghosts (who you can't normally engage in combat but they can still kill you handily, by the way). Far along into the city I discovered a fog door, which almost always means there's a boss battle on the other side. Awesome! So I go in and go down a very long winding stone staircase. Then the stairs stop and an endless pit looms below. Uh oh, a weird dead-end! So I climb all the way back up the stairs and you can't go back out the doorway that you just walked through. What?! So I had no choice but to jump to my death. YOU DIED.

I turned to the internet to see what the hell just happened. Turns out you have to get and equip a ring that's the reward for killing another boss enemy VERY far away from that pit and then jump to your death- only this time it won't kill you, you'll instead have to fight a hard boss called the Four Kings. Now I've owned this game for a couple years now and though I was able to eventually win it there's just no way I would have ever figured out some of this stuff. I'm all for things taking a bit of brain to solve ("GRUMBLE, GRUMBLE.." in level 7 of NES Zelda was an infamous puzzle for me back in the day for example)but this was a bit much in my opinion.

Or another encounter I had, also in that same city: I somehow managed to stay alive long enough to get to the top of a roof where a guy in red robes was. I talked to him, and hey he didn't kill me- cool! But I didn't really GET anywhere with him. I checked out 'ol internets' again and it said he was supposed to give me some sort of item to get past another area. So I went back and tried talking to him again. Nothing special, no items were had. So I figured, well, I guess I have to try and kill him to get the item. And kill him I did- only to later find out that had I completed some other section of the game FIRST and THEN come back here he would have gladly given me the item and still be alive to set up camp at the Firelink Shrine to sell me stuff for the rest of the game. Son of a bitch!

Combat-wise the game is very good and it's true even at level 90-something if you're careless an enemy 50 levels below you can kick you ass and kill you. There are many many types of weapons and armor to collect, upgrade, and use, but there are some types that I don't think many will ever WANT to use themselves. For example, there are I think only 2 whips in the entire game and sadly even when fully upgraded they're terrible vs. almost everything you have to fight. Daggers are great weapons but if you want to try an interesting build like dual weilding them all the time you won't like the results too much.

Why is that? Well I think the game pretty much forces you to use a shield for 90% of the time. My method of playing- which from the looks of it is the way most 'regular' people play- is to shield block an enemy's attack and counter with your own attack. The thing is, I can't imagine trying to go up against some of these bastards without the ability to block their devastatingly-powerful attacks. I mean technically-speaking you CAN block a battleaxe or fireball with your butter knives but you'll still take a lot of damage from it. A lot of enemies even have special moves that are completely unblockable and you'll never know it until the big YOU DIED pops up again.

While it might seem that I'm all piss and vinegar over Dark Souls I do truly like this game and would consider it a classic, even with its (minor?) flaws. Like NES Metroid, there's a huge feeling of wonderment, exploration, and solitude that just really can't be beat by games nowadays. Some compare this to Dragon's Dogma- a game a personally enjoy more- but still I give this brutal puppy a solid 4/5 stars.
rows.title: Excellent but ultimately too hard for its own good

rows.text:
In 1792 Captain Collier (Patrick Allen) is tasked with investigating reports of smuggling in the small town of Dymchurch on the English coast. He and his small band of sailors have with them a mulatto (Milton Reid). He is a mute. Years before his tongue was cut out by the pirate Captain Clegg for attacking Clegg’s wife. Captain Collier saved the mulatto from certain death when he was abandoned on an uninhabited island. Collier now keeps him as a slave, sort of a human bloodhound.

Collier has the local ale house ransacked looking for contraband alcohol. The ale house is run by Rash (Martin Benson) and his ward Imogène (Yvonne Romain). The mulatto finds a hidden cellar. Unbeknownst to Collier the ale house cellar is connected by a secret passageway to the home of Jeremiah Mipps (Michael Ripper), the coffin maker. Wine is hidden in the coffins. The building serves as the smugglers' headquarters. The parson of the village is Dr. Blyss (Peter Cushing). He is the leader of the smugglers. When the mulatto sees Blyss he inexplicably attacks him and is subdued by the sailors.

That night, the smugglers move their stash of contraband wine to a nearby windmill and on for shipment. The squire's son Harry (Oliver Reed) is shot in the arm by Collier and wounded. Harry is in love with Imogène. They are secretly engaged. Meanwhile, at the ale house, Rash kills one of the sailors to prevent him from notifying Collier about the smuggling operation. The mulatto finds the key to his chains on the dead soldier and frees himself. The mulatto then goes to the churchyard and breaks into Clegg's grave. The Mulatto makes another attempt on Blyss’s life. Collier is now suspicious of Blyss.

A small fire at the parson’s house results in Rash finding Clegg's last will and testament. He learns from it that Imogène is Clegg's daughter. He attempts to exploit that knowledge by forcing himself on Imogene. She escapes and flees to Blyss’s home. Blyss and Harry both tell her they already know about her heritage. Harry confronts Rash. He is arrested by Collier when the captain notices his bandaged arm. Harry is taken away to Collier's ship. He escapes when the Marsh Phantoms appear, distracting the sailors. The phantoms seem to know the right time to make an appearance.

“Night Creatures” AKA “Captain Clegg” is one of Hammer’s forgotten films. It was released in 1962. The film was directed by Peter Graham Scott and stars Peter Cushing, Yvonne Romain, Patrick Allen, Oliver Reed, Martin Benson and Michael Ripper (who steals the show). “Night Creatures” was mislabeled as a horror movie. Technically it is an adventure story. Even so, it’s got that Hammer flair. Great period costumes and sets. Good acting. The story is basic. The phantoms are well done. Hammer did a good job of creating the ghost horse and rider effect. Unfortunately it’s only seen in a few places and for a short time but when it’s used it adds a lot to the movie. They are actually cool looking.

If you like period pieces or love Peter Cushing than this is something you may be interested in. Don’t think horror movie, think Eroll Flynn or Douglas Fairbanks but with a lot less swashbuckling.

“Captain Clegg and the Night Creatures” is the name of a band in Rob Zombie’s 2009 movie Halloween II.
rows.title: Perish all who betray Captain Clegg.

rows.text:
There are technically 5 games on here, and I'll go over them one at a time explaining why this is the best game ever and you should sell Modern Warfare 12 or whatever it is now and get this instead. So let's begin shall we?

Half Life 2/Portal: They're good.

Now onto the REAL star of the show here, the highly addicting TF2! The mentally challenged dislike this game on first impression basis because it looks too cartoony and they want another brown/grey grizzled average American Joe Hero to play as instead. Let them rot in their purgatory of mediocrity, Team Fortress 2 is a bright and unique looking game akin to a violent Pixar movie where 9 mercenaries with unique abilities kill each other for unimportant reasons. Aaaaaand the mercenaries are:

Scout: A hotheaded Boston with a baseball bat shotgun and pistol, players can use his speed to their advantage which is about all the Scout has as a use, since he can't take very much damage before dying. People who are really good with the Scout game use his nimbleness to ambush enemy teams by jumping in and terrorizing the enemy team while they frantically try to relatiate to the little Boston Bastard. On the flipside people who suck with the Scout are usually just run around like everyone else without using agility and get their asses kicked in a matter of seconds.

Soldier: A stereotypical American similar to R. Lee Ermey, this guy is armed with a shovel, shotgun, and a rocketlauncher, and is usually played by people who like to blitzkrieg into a game blowing everything up. A good Soldier is a cheap bastard who jumps around raping everything with his Medic butt-buddy, Soldier's aren't hard to play so generally angry frustrated 10 year olds like to helm the Soldier for compensation purposes, and even a really bad Soldier is still a bit dangerous unless you know how to sneak up on em.

Pyro: A gas-mask clad pyromaniac(duh) weilds a fire axe, a shotgun, and a flamethrower, and they can f*** s*** up at close quarters, but if you take 'em into long distance areas they are quite useless. Despite Pyro's being in the 'attack' class I've found them to be most useful for defending, and a good Pyro has to master the art of ambushing, so they can pop out of a corner to set an unsuspecting sucker on fire. A lame Pyro has no concept of distance and usually chases someone 20 ft away with a flamethrower with a range of about 3ft, and they just end up looking silly.

Demo-man: A black Scottish alcoholic psyclops (that's a mouthful) armed with a bottle of booze, a 'nade launcher and a sticky bombs, making the Demo-man similar to the Soldier with a penchant for blowing things up, however unlike the Soldier the Demo-man isn't a cheap little turd because he's often left vulernable when confronted at close range. Good Demo-men set up booby traps with the sticky bombs and make a living hell for the enemy team, while bad Demo-men fire around randomly praying something will die, which usually ends up being them.

Heavy: The obese bald Russian on the cover weilds a minigun, a shotgun(real original) and his fists to bash puny cowards into oblivion. The Heavy is slow but powerful, similar to a tank, and is useful in both defense/offense. Crappy Heavies often forget that they can die and jump right into the middle of a hot zone and absorb all of the bullets. A good Heavy hopefully has a medic to back him up, is fully aware of things going on around him in case the Medic is getting attacked or a Spy is sneaking up on him. A bad Heavy (about 90% of them) run around in the open like giddy school girls before taking a Sniper bullet to the head or a knife in the back.

Engineer: For those who REALLY can't aim, the Engineer is a Texan weilding a shotgun (again?), wrench, and a toolbox to build teleporters, a dispenser, and of course the sentry gun. Crap Engineers leave their sentrys unattended in obvious spots that's easy to take out, while good ones stick to their guns (PUN HYUCK HYUCK) and keep alert for Spies or Demo-men who can take sentrys out pretty easily. Engineers are all about location.

Medic: Most underrated class, the Medic is a German ex-Nazi equipped with a needle gun, a bonesaw, and a healgun that, you guessed it, heals teammates. Medics can't take much health but slowly regenerate health and are most useful on the front lines healing the more powerful classes, so in the middle of it all a good Medic is nimble and heals everyone while a s*** Medic will only heal his Heavy butt-buddy and stand in one place begging the enemy team to take him out. If a team sucks they'll usually be bitching amongst each other over who has to be a Medic.

Sniper: My personal favorite, the Snoipah is an Australian Woody-look alike who weilds a sniper rifle, a machete, and a sub-machine gun. Snipers are either completely useless wastes of space or really good at supressing an attack and keeping the enemy from advancing. Usually they're piss-poor at aiming and you can always tell someone doesn't know what they're doing if they pull the SMG out and meet you in the middle of a battlefield because they think they're playing Call of Doodie. Good Snipers stay hidden in shadows, usually cover a busy area, and are quick and efficient with the rifle at taking people out.

Spy: Similar to the Sniper where they're either completely useless or really ass-kicking, the Spy is a monotone Frenchman who can disguise himself as an enemy teammate and sneak around them undetected. The Spy is equipped with a magnum, a butterfly knife (which is as useful as a toothpick if you don't backstab with it), a disguise kit to select which class to look like, and an electro-sapper to destroy the Engineer's stuff. Spies that are useless usually don't cloak themselves, stand in front of the enemy long enough for them to suspect something, and generally are only useful as bullet absorbers. Good spies don't go out into the middle of the battlefield and stay in the shadows preying on people who's backs are turned, and constantly moving.

And that's it for the classes, they all balance each other out nicely too. For example Engineers can stop Scouts from entering an area, Scouts can easily take out a Demo-man, and a Demo-man can take out an Engineer's sentry with ease. The one thing I don't really like though is the lack of level variety, all the levels are basically just abandoned industrial warehouses. 2fort is the most popular one and a sniper's paradise, Dustbowl is the second most popular and ends pretty quickly when one team can't get their s*** together, Well and Granary both have teams on opposite sides fighting for more control points, Gravel Pit is similar to Dustbowl where one team usually wipes the other team out, and no one plays Hydro.

It's worth noting that I've never played TF2 on computer, those players tend to bemoan the 'good old days' when TF2 was simpler/cleaner to look at and not a hat simulater, but there is also more variety in weapons as well as levels, so it's a mixed blessing I suppose. I've been playing TF2 like a crack addict for the last year now and it hasn't shown one sign of getting old for me so I'd heartily recommend it to all 0 of my fans reading this. Oh yeah, the other games on the Orange Box are good too I guess.
rows.title: Warning: Extremely Addicting; Proceed with Caution

rows.text:
I eagerly awaited Mass Effect 2, preordered it, and counted down the days for 3 months. Overall, it's a good experience. I think most of the gripes I had with the game might be due to overthinking everything, but some changes really annoyed me. Minor spoilers ahead.

GRAPHICS: 8/10
The graphics got a major overhaul this time around. The lighting is superb. The faces during conversation look just as awful as ever, and it's difficult to spot expressions or emotions, just like in the first one. The camera during conversations is a lot better, though. It moves with you, shakes during explosions, an has some interesting effects that make the conversations seem a lot more than just two heads talking. The environments are a lot more varied, and by a lot more varied, I really mean a lot more. You can actually distinguish between planets, unlike the first one, in which planets differed only in ground color and number/placement of annoying mountains(exploration). Some armor textures are lacking, and there's a lot of clipping near the shoulderpads...still.

Of course, graphics don't really matter at all since Mass Effect shouldn't about the visual experience.

SOUND: 6/10
The original ME soundtrack with all its mesmerizing techno beats and synth is pretty much gone. You can still play them in your cabin, but only your cabin. I didn't find anything memorable about the ME2 soundtrack at all. The voice acting is as good as ever, of course. All the guns sound alike for each category.

There's nothing really unique about the sound.

STORYLINE: 6/10
Really disappointing, in short. This is probably due to the fact that ME2 is the second installment of a trilogy, where everything is built up for the final denouement in the third part. Also, I didn't read any of the novels, which the game makes several references to. Nothing really happens to advance the overall story until the very end. The first Mass Effect would be entirely unrelated to the second one if it weren't for the choice decision mechanic. Oh, look at that, Wrex is still alive (or dead), great. You still have the same quest and most of the dialogue is still the same. Oh hey, the Council is back, and their the same douchebags they were before, or hey, the Alliance took over and now they're the douchebags the Council was 2 years back. There are some good twists here and there, but all of them require an intimate knowledge of the first game (which I played for over 100 hours and enjoyed greatly). Mass Effect 2's storyline isn't that accessible to the new gamer and your enemy, the Reapers, aren't revealed until the very end and you really don't care about them at all during the game. The emotional bonds you make with your squad is not as good, either. There are too many cliches. While in the first one, the characters had interesting backgrounds (disillusioned police officer, Pilgrimage, tortured biotic, person-not-yet-respected-because-of-her-age (really couldn't come up with anything there)), ME2 traverses the well-beaten path (badass who curses too much and wants to kill everything to an extreme degree, goody-two-shoes, mercenary who's doing it just for the exorbitant pay). There are some interesting characters like Doctor Mordin, who struggles with morality and spirituality, and Jacob, who has a deadbeat father, but overall, the characters are unsatisfying. There are some sequences in which you question philosophy and morality, but generally it's the same old "gray area, better good" stuff. Also, the "robot" (no spoilers) has a great personality. It might be that the character's struggles are too general. For example, the things I enjoyed the most were little tidbits and details like Garrus' fistfight with a female turian (on a side note, Garrus doesn't talk to you at all...annoying since he was a well developed character), the GameSpot employee parody ([..]), and the C-Sec officer who claimed that "Sir Isaac Newton is the deadliest son of a bitch in space." ([..]) It's moments like these that really make the Mass Effect 2 world sparkle.

Overall, you really need to have experienced the first Mass Effect to enjoy the second...and this is coming from a guy who read all the Codex entries, got 100% completion, and pretty much tried all the paths. The storyline isn't that good, either, and the characters in ME2 are not as compelling as the characters in ME1. If you played the first one, the references to the first are satisfying for the most part (they exist, that's good enough for me).

GAMEPLAY: 8/10
Being the hardcore RPG fan that I am, I'm even surprised at myself for giving the gameplay a 8 out of 10. Even so, you don't have to be an RPG fan to realize the humongous lack of customization in this game. Having bars to tweak the colors and patterns of your armor is useless and does not make up for the scrapped looting system. The only reason why I was able to endure this travesty was that I was had the pre-order armor, Dragon Age Armor, Cerberus DLC armor, and DrPepper armor (Don't judge me). Otherwise, the armor in this game is severely lacking. Emphasis on severely. It doesn't help that you can somehow kiss and drink water through your helmets. Scratch that, this is Mass Effect 2 and Liara's lips are obviously phasing through the glass, so it does make sense. Speaking of inconsistencies, they added ammo with an unconvincing codex explanation. Inconsistencies aside, thermal clips do not make good gameplay sense. I have to walk around looking for thermal clips that sometimes disappear and my sniper rifle only has 10 shots? Of course, one can argue that ammo adds to the tactical gameplay. This game also feels far more like a generic shooter with really bad cover mechanics and a distinct lack of weapons. There are 2-3 weapons for each category and about 7 heavy weapons not including DLC. The shooter component of the game still isn't very good despite Bioware's attempts to broaden the accessibility. However, that is another mechanic I can overlook since ME2 is still about the strategy. If you aren't playing Soldier, you will have to meticulously pick your team and skills for each situation, prioritize, and use the tactics menu a lot. Also, in normal difficulty, I could easily forgive the lousy cover system that pancaked me to the side of the wall, or vaulted me into enemy fire, but it's only on Insane difficulty where I really felt it. Many a time, I was launched or put into a compromising position and subsequently killed in an instant and had to restart the entire, 15 minute long battle over again (you can't save in the midst of battle, of course). The shooting was awful, but blowing up robot body parts was pretty interesting, and the barrier/shield/armor/bullet type was a great strategic addition to the game. The mini-games are a lot more interesting this time around, with a cool circuit game and a clever hacking game that actually looks like coding. Also, while the armor upgrades and armor pieces are really crappy (as I previously mentioned), the weapon system is nothing short of amazing. It encourages (or forces you, depending on how much you like the mini-game) to explore the galaxy and really emulates the RPG feeling of leveling up really well. On Insanity mode, I struggled through the early levels by taking 50 shots to kill a standard infantry, but by the time I had all the upgrades, I demolished everything in my path. That sense of improvement is what defines an RPG, and so I grudgingly give the gameplay an 8, despite so many shortcomings and what one can perceive to be the selling out of the Mass Effect franchise.

Overall, the gameplay was watered down, the shooting mechanics still suck, but in a different way, the RPG elements are mostly gone, but still done pretty well. The tactical element of the game is even better. The game is very buggy, sometimes frustratingly buggy. The "impact" of your choices short-term is very blunt. Get your team's loyalty and survive the mission, or be a jackass and have everyone die.

FUN: 10/10
Of course, none of this crap matters, because in the end, it all boils down to how much fun one had with the game. Despite all the changes, I had a lot of fun with this game and logged over 100 hours on two different characters. It's also worth noting that it's really fun and unrealistic that you can hook up with most of the women in your crew. If only dating were that easy.

Mass Effect 2 is a great game that's very different from the first. It's definitely worth buying despite some drawbacks. It may not be the best Bioware has to offer, but it's still pretty damn good.
rows.title: Not what I was expecting

rows.text:
I'm very disappointed! I was looking forward reading this sequel to Phoenix Dark Messiah which was a fun post apocalyptic novel with Commando mixed themes in it. The start of the book Summer Song after the events of Dark Messiah leaving Trench aka The Phoenix with no reason only to stay in another town. Trench continues he quest to return to New York hoping his son & wife are still alive after WWIII broke out.

First page Trench enters a town to get food, ammo and gas however quickly learns the town's people capture people and steal whatever they own. Trench barely escapes and is saved by old man who takes him to Las Vegas which is owned by The Sheik. After learning a wanted bounty is on The Phoenix, Sheik makes Trench a deal to enter in his death sport or be sold off to his enemy. Trench enters in a Death Race like sport with muscle cars covered in armour and weapons. Trench wins the race taking out all the other drivers then makes a escape and almost getting killed again when a fighter jet shows up and fires a missile hitting his car and crashing into a tunnel yet somehow walking away unburned or uninjured.

And this is where things get messed up. Trench is told by a group of people mostly women called Earthwomb stating he is the choice one to fight the evil and to proof this he must have sex with Earthwomb leader and then the story starts jumping all over the damn place! This is one thing I can't stand what some authors will do they'll write the first book right but when it comes to 2nd or 3rd novel they'll screw something up! I'm not going to lie there were parts which I felt would fix the messy beginning of this story and would have a nice ending to make me read the 3rd book but things fall apart! Also I hated how the writer kept using the words "slut" & "whore" when both good & bad characters are talking to women. Is it that hard to say woman that much in the story? Does every male character in this story have to always acted like prick?

"Sign" Despite the nice detail in the story with a very interesting setting and characters this sequel fails to have all the fun in Dark Messiah.
This was also published on the same year 1987 was the author in a rush to get this sequel done or didn't know how to make good as his first book?
One last thing the villain lacks background I was wondering how Dark Messiah started this whole war but each time the book goes back to him, he keeps stating he is getting stronger and building his army out of mutated children and watching people being killed in gory ways.
I'm very disappointed!
rows.title: A Total Mess!!!

rows.text:
I started Panther across the sky by Lon Brett Coon with pretty high hopes. The story itself is pretty well written and there is a lot going for it. There were also a few things that didn’t work for me, too. To begin with, I’ll focus on some of the positives. Ever since graduating college nearly two decades ago I’ve worked with teens and nearly always worked with/near Native American youth. The opening scene with Makya felt very believable (kind of, more below) as he raged and acted out the anger welling up within him. Basically, he goes out and gets drunk with some total strangers who tell him a tale (the central story of the book) which makes the novel 1 part Everyman story, 1 part morality tale, and 1 part Native American Princess Bride… which is cool, but can rub many people the wrong way unless the story is well-wrapped in genre fiction (within religious tales and morality stories, however, the story-in-a-story motif can be common.)
The opening scene features some great skill by Coon in demonstrating intense language that demonstrates emotion and action. However, some of the details don’t make sense. Coon beautifully shows Makya’s poverty by referencing an older model television and clothing with holes… but then his mother Nengala comes and gives him a hug because her angry son was so upset that he busted the tv, mirror, and anything glass. Her actions defy the beautiful scene Coon setup—the proximity to our modern culture are what make some of those scenes unbelievable: it’s easier to lose the suspension of disbelief when we are already affluent with the culture. To be fair, when Coon writes the rest of the story—the a historical fiction story of Tecumseh—the writing is much more on point. It’s easier to stay in the story and maintain the disbelief that the author asks us to buy into.
The reemergence of Makya at the end feels heavy handed, like the author finally shows his cards and tells us his message in explicit detail and so it didn’t work for me… an angry boy speaking words as if they were pulled from a Wikipedia post in preparation for a high school history exam felt more farfetched than that same boy being thrown back in time a few hundred years to experience a moral lesson from his people’s history. It kind of ruined the story in my opinion. Truly, my only problems were with the characters and scenes in the prologue and epilogue—which are basically disconnected to the rest of the story (except that it connects the moral lesson to a modern age).
The story is a solid tale—just don’t read the prologue or epilogue unless you want to feel talked down to and led towards the exact conclusions the author wants you to arrive at. This is Coon’s first story and it is a fine tale and otherwise well done; I’ve no doubt that he will learn a few things as he continues to write and that his next book just may be a perfect read.
I got a copy of the book for free in exchange for an honest review.
rows.title: inside the inkwell review

rows.text:
(SPOILER ALERT),.but does it really matter???
I hate the star system because as they say one man's trash is another man's.. well let's just be honest here...trash..
To be or not to be British is the question but what is the answer???? For the life of me, I can not understand how a movie that is obviously shot in the UK that also features British actors, would try to moonlight as being in the USA?
Their awful American accents are abysmal at best.
If the cast would try as hard to act as they do trying to sound American, then this film would be...nawww who am I kidding?, it would be like if you sprayed perfume on a pile of crap, not only would it still stink but it would still be crap.
Why then do some of the students and teachers still have their British accent? ( which at times reminds me of an Aussie accent, In fact, I thought that this was an Australian film but I read that it was British)
QUESTION: What rhymes with Cupid but begins with the letter S?
Figure out that riddle and you'll understand the rest of this review..
This holiday crapfest begins looking like the beginning of a really cheap looking Disney movie.
A semi-animated book tells the story of the mythological creature called Cupid. However, they twist and bend the true lore to their will to fit the story-line for this movie.
Here it is in a nutshell... A Valentines Day version of "Mean Girls" with a killer Cupid, where the lead mean girl Elise, is far more scary and sinister than the actual killer Cupid himself???
First, we get to see some total douchebag who is two timing his wife and is boring his date to death..literally..
His daughter?? who looks a bit too old to be his daughter, more like his wife or sister, comes into the restaurant where said bad date was taking place, looking completely disheveled and devastated and is basically cock-blocking her father's date.
Meanwhile, his date excuses herself to the Ladies room, where she climbs out of a window to escape her nightmare date.
Unfortunately for her, Cupid just happens to be flying by and sends an arrow right through her eye.
About this time seems a good time for his very mature looking daughter to come clean. "Umm.. yeah.. dad.. ummm.. when you stepped out on mom, I was mad at you so what else could I do but summon an evil demon to get even with you?.. how could I have ever known that it could ever have gone this badly"?
Father and daughter, hand in hand, try to outrun the evil killer Cupid but to no avail, he promptly catches up with them, shoots douchebag dad in the leg and then impale's dear daughter, eye first, right through the head via the eye on dear old dad's leg arrow.
Fast forward....
A homely young outcast Faye, is bullied by a bunch of mean girls led by Elise, because Faye's slutty mother is sleeping with Elise's father.
Obviously, Elise doesn't take too well to the fact that Faye's mother is whoring around with her father.
Elise relentlessly bullies Faye.
Meanwhile... Faye has a secret crush on her sexy English teacher Mr. Jones (who used to be a dropout doctor but we learn that much later in the film). Mr. Jones is a great example of terrible acting because he is too busy trying to force an American accent.
So...what's a girl to do when she has a secret crush on her teacher? Why, pull out her handy dandy black magic book, that incidentally happens to be the very same ancient animated looking book from the opening of the film..but I digress...
Faye in all of her charming innocence decides to cast a love spell on the sexy Mr. Jones.
When the wicked Elise gets wind of her schoolgirl crush, she hatches a diabolical plan to get even with her for her mother's extracurricular activities with her father.
Elise steals Mr. Jones phone, sends her texts asking her to send him naughty pics and arranges a date. Of course, unbeknownst to Mr. Jones, that Faye's passion burns hot and heavy for him, she plants a kiss firmly on his lips while Else and her evil minions cackle with laughter as they film the whole mortifying event.
From this point, I'm just going to fast forward.. which you might want to do yourself with this movie or maybe even skip it altogether...
Elise arranges a very interesting video nasty that features ( much to their shock and dismay) both Faye and Mr. Jones engaged in that fateful kiss.
Well.. a totally embarrassed Faye runs out of her secret classroom rendezvous but not before getting tripped, only to burst through the door right into a hallway filled with pics of her more private.. shall we say..attributes?
Well.. the girls get into a big tiff and try to rip each other's hair and eyes out. After the fight is broken up, they are sent to Principal McHottie's office ( also known as my future husband) to be severely punished by cancelling the school Valentines Dance and having to stay for detention to clean up the decorations.
Well what is a scorned young girl to do?, why pick up that little book of black magic and summon death himself this time.
Well.. it seems that not only is Death British but he is trying oh so hard through his vocoder to also sound like he is from ancient times and failing miserably ( bless his heart).
Death seems to be very well versed by his legal team to make sure that Faye is very clear on the terms, rules and consequences of their unholy agreement.
Basically, if Faye can't have love then no one in the school should have love either. She wants to destroy love.
Well I guess Death has better things to do then deal with foolish spoiled mortals, so he gives her a 24 hour curse that ends at Midnight on Valentines Day.
Fast forward again.... One of the mean girl minions ( does it really matter who?.. but she did get to keep her real accent, if that helps?) meets Cupid face to face and he shoots an arrow through the bathroom door right through the side of her head.. then he gets another mean girl and makes her face a human vase for all of the lovely roses he crams violently down her throat.
I forget which mean girl gets turned into a skin cookie factory?? but luckily this ancient killer demon just so happens to carry his very own set of heart shaped cookie cutters??
Cupid even had time to bake a batch of whoremade mean girl cookies.. in-between his killing spree.. of course.
Fast forward to the detention cleaning up the decorations party, there is a mysterious plate of Valentines Day cookies . Mysteriously, no one knows who made them, so of course they promptly gobble them all up. They don't seem too phased that they taste like chicken???
Fun Fact: I guess human skin must taste like chicken.. ??
Elise gets smart with a teacher and leaves detention only to escape to her smokin' hot boyfriend's waiting car, that incidentally won't start.
Now my gorgeous husband Principal McHottie, has a tendency to overact and so he gets a bit psycho but not before they all spot something in the sky..
It's a bird.. it's a plane.. no it's the killer demon Cupid sent to... Wait!!! .. what was he sent to do again? Destroy love? Well.. I guess Cupid must have not gotten the memo from Death and he thought it said KILL EVERYONE!
Sadly, Principal McHottie gets a famous, Cupid special arrow to the eye ( don't worry it's only a movie, he's only an actor and we are still going to be married..I'll send you all our gift registry).
Then Elise's hot boyfriend ( hey I'm engaged not dead..I can still look) gets a few poisoned arrows to his leg and back.. Oh wait, did I forget to mention that Cupid's arrows are poisonous? They must still be the same ones that his mother Venus tampered with at the faux Disney opening of the movie...???
Fast forward because I'm getting foggy at this point.. Cupid kills a bunch of mean girls and a mean boy..oh and a few teachers too.
Faye has an ooopsie "did I do that"? Urkle moment as the demon she summoned decides to kill everyone in the school.
Elise discovers that Faye is moonlighting as an amateur witch and promptly scolds her. Faye finds out that her super whiny friend (who by the way got to keep his British accent too) got shot by Cupid's arrow so they must rush him to the science lab.
Remember the sexy doctor dropout turned English teacher Dr. Jones? Well.. it just so happens that he must also watch "The Walking Dead" because he knows that in order to stop the infection from spreading, that they have to chop off Faye's friend's arm but not before he tells her that he has been in love with her since 5th grade.
Even though Faye follows that sentiment with "you're like my little brother" but once we chop off that arm, I'll take you on a date...because you know a date with crazy amateur witch Faye, who summoned a demon to kill everyone she knows and now he has to lose an arm..is a great trade off right???
Wait.. hold up!!! how are you going to chop off an infected arm in the Science Lab? why with this very conveniently placed hack saw of course.. Everyone knows that the perfect accouterment to a Science Lab is a rusty old hacksaw...duh???
Mr. Jones asks him the million dollar question "do you trust me"?
right before he slowly saws off his arm. No one thought to step back a little so they all got drenched with buckets of blood.
Just when you think the cheese whiz can't get any cheesier, think again. Death by greeting cards, yes when you care enough to send the scary best you need to beef up on your Valentines greeting cards that also not only double as Ninja throwing stars but also can slice open a mean boy's throat.. who knew? I thought maybe he'd get a nasty paper cut at best. Well.. you know those crazy kids at Hell-mark.. what will they think of next?
Fast forward..I think..
About this time, a teacher and Mr. Jones makes out in front of poor broken hearted Faye but it's ok guys because they say "sorry" to her..??? So you know that makes everything right??
but Faye is "so over everything".. so we're all good here ( thumbs up!).
Even though Witchcraft and summoning ancient demons has taught Faye and the gang nothing, Faye decides to once again summon Death..
Well death has about had it with this little annoying bitch and he pretty much tells her nothing can stop the situation and she must see the 24 hour curse through. Well.. luckily for everybody there is only 2 more minutes left until midnight and the end of Cupid's curse.
Well.. Death himself said it best "a lot can happen in two minutes" and it was just enough time to bring about the brutal and untimely death of queen bee mean girl herself Elise.. YAY!!!
No one ever seemed phased by a killer demonic Cupid or Death HIMSELF showing up or pretty much the gruesome deaths of all of their classmates and faculty...???
Elise has a .. wait! "i'm not quite dead yet Monty Python moment , only to be knocked back down by a now very confident Faye. This also did not seem to phase anyone either???
Pretty much after that, the movie just ends abruptly not because there might be a sequel but because they either ran out of film or just simply got bored and said.."the hell with this!".???
So if you've had enough American cheese and you want to sample some good old foreign cheese, then "Cupid" has plenty of cheese for you to indulge yourself with. Oh and while you are at it, you might want to try some of Mother Cupid's good ole fashioned cookies because chicken and cheese pairs really well...
MY TAKE ON THIS HORRORDAY MESS?
the acting is pretty bad but the accents are worse.
the story line is boring unoriginal and pretty disorganized..
There is ZERO character development, except for the tiny backstory of Faye..if anyone cares..? So you basically could care less about any of the characters and you root for them all to die ( except Principal McHottie who I didn't want to die but rather LOVE me forever!)
If you are really bored and I mean REALLY desperately bored out of your skull.. then you might enjoy watching this big old, chicken-flavored cheesefest!
HAPPY VD DAY!!!!
#cupid #cupidmovie #cupidmovie2020 #valentinesday #valentinesdayhorror
rows.title: TO BE ..OR NOT TO BE.. BRITISH?

rows.text:
I thought this was well written, the story-line flowed, and I had an actual feel for both heroes (which isn't that common, let me tell you). The reason I found it a bit disappointing was some of the glaring old misogyny hangups. I don't need strong women in an M/M Romance. I understand that by its very nature, M/M romance is going to focus on male-male interaction. Okay. But when the ONLY female character is presented as a slut, given no realistic motivation for her actions, and is also TREATED like a slut and dismissed by the other characters, I have a problem. The entire Sarah thing felt tacked on, entirely unnecessary, and the way she behaved made no sense when we consider who she was presented to be in book one. It was as if she was turned into a interfering tramp just to get a half chapter scene of jealousy from Logan. Then he does the slut shaming by accusing her of trying to win Kyle "with a set of tits".
Yeah, no thanks.
If the only female characters you're capable of writing are the elderly mother (non threatening) and the interfering whore (artificially threatening), than please don't bother at all.
PS: the manage scene with the woman Logan picked up in the bar? Wow. Was it your intention to have the heroes come off as bastards, or was that just a side effect?
rows.title: Okay story, good writing. Same old dismissive crap with female characters though

rows.text:
This wasn't the worst thing I've read but it certainly wasn't the best either. The sex scenes are not very well written in my opinion. After the first 2 or 3 I started skimming them. Too much porn talk (are there people that really talk like that during sex???) I felt like I was reading a script for a porn and after the first scene they all just ran together and was the same thing over and over. Not hot at all.

I also am having a hard time liking the charcters. Talon is an asshole! Yeah he has a terrible past and has reasons for being aloof and defensive but he sure makes it hard to like him. And Jade doesnt know about his past and just lets him treat her like that. If a guy said all those things to me and then said "i want you to suck my cock" I'd tell the asshole to take a hike. I mean, what the fuck? If she already knew about everything in his past or at least had a pretty good idea it would be one thing, but she doesnt so it just makes her sound like a twit.

If I hadnt already purchased the second book when i purchased this one i wouldn't continue to read this series. I still might not.
rows.title: I wanted so bad to like this book.

rows.text: Madame de Pompadour just like the prick of a French King's (Louis XV) other concubines was nothing but a whore and who should not be celebrated for being a survivor but rather a prostitute! The Queen was not a bore but rather a lonely woman who deeply loved her husband despite the fact that he was a bastard to her and cheated on her repeatedly with low lives such as this whore! The only good thing that I can say about her is that she was cordial to the long suffering Queen Marie Leszczyñska when his other whores had snubbed her, and the Queen herself is said to have said of her "If there must be a mistress, better her than any other."
rows.title: Madame de Pompadour was a whore

rows.text:
In yet another blunder for the New 52, Geoff Johns (Remember when he did awesome stuff like JSA or Green Lantern?) and Jim Lee (Who designed every crappy new costume) hooked up Superman and Wonder Woman. Because they both have powers, and the staff of DC is composed of very immature 12 year olds. So lets drop and degrade Lois, so we can have them fly around and fight and kiss.

Following this revelation of pure shallowness, they decided to give Superman and Wonder Woman their own book. For the first time in decades- Wonder Woman has a second comic, and all they had to do was attach her to Superman to do it because Wonder Woman was last interesting in 1947. Hoping to capture the brain-dead teenage market, they decided to make this book try and appeal to the Twilight crowd. Team Lois or Team Wonder Woman? How about Team FireDidio?

Chales Soule trained as a lawyer, so his lack of a soul probably helped him pen this dreck. He is probably has too much on his plate and knows that this is not going to win any awards because it is blatantly a corporate created idea, so he dropped this hot mess after 12 issues. After a crossover orchestrated by Scott Lobdell. Let's take a look at the title for this volume: Power Couple. Like a Hollywood Power Couple, aren't they clever?

Superman is badly characterized here. He is a douche bag, like most New 52 heroes. Because Batman is cool and everything should be all like Batman. Variety is not for us! Wonder Woman is the angry warrior woman who comes from a line of sailer-raping baby murders who think their culture is awesome! She's feminist because she treats men like crap. He and his new trick fight Zod and Faora (MOS tie-in! Gotta see that movie where Nolan and Snyder piss over Siegal and Shuster's graves!) and some Greek Gods, the trailer trash of pantheons. It got old after a few issues and I decided that the money could better be spent on Sugar and Spike back issues so I stopped doing more than flipping through it. It ends with Wonder Woman being totally better (more feminism!) and Soule ripping a scene out of Dark Knight Returns. Next its Doomed, and that was written with Lobdell so skip that if you have any self respect left.

Tony Daniel's art is decent, he's not addicted to cross hatching so there's that. Art cannot completely overcome the bad story. It should come as no shock that I do not recommend this. If you must buy a new 52 Superman story that go with Unchained or Morrison's Action Comics or Geoff John's new run on Superman with fug art. Or donate the cash to an animal shelter because...puppies are there and puppies are awesome.
rows.title: Super-Douche and Wonder Bitch

rows.text:
it just get too absurd and comical when the detective has a a bizarre sexual fetish of being dominated by a fat and hideous old women. They made the other female characters in the story much prettier than you would usually find in upstate New York I live in nyc but have a hunting lodge estate in upstate the women are not attractive at all. They could have at least made the dominatrix attractive like some of the others sexualized characters.
beyond that its interesting bc in the first few episodes you really hate this women bc she lies to her husband and family and says she was a whore and you just thunk she's the typical lower class promiscuous women who eventually finds a sucker to marry her and take care of her after deceiving him about everything. some things come to light through flashbacks and the detectives investigating that make her appear more sympathetic, but not much. She was still a slutty girl who behaved inn a trashy way but its avg teenage trashy stuff..the kind of lies most women who are not ladies have. mainly this interesting contrived conspiracy of child abuse with upper middle class rich people from new but established American families emerges. it could be interesting but that all falls apart and the plot twist at the end is kind of lame,.

Once they established that who dynamic they should have left it alone instead of it becomes some weird sub plot between the detective and his self entitled wife and her.
