rows.text:
Dick Grayson is my favorite DC hero, as nightwing and also my favorite batman. So I was a little upset when I found out that in the new 52 he wasn't a hero anymore but a secret agent, I had heard a lot of great things about it so I decided to give it a go and found myself presently surprised, I did actually enjoy the first issues but the quality really started to drop after the robin war issue, this series just felt like it didn't know what it wanted to be, dc kept trying to convince us how high the stakes were if Dick didn't succeed but all throughout the series I just didn't feel it, Dick was also very sexulized in this series and I didn't have a problem with that however those scenes made me cringe so bad that I had to put the comic down for about 10 minutes, it was just so over the top and it wouldn't had been as bad if this had happened in every 5 issues or something but no it pretty much happened in every single issue, Dick was sexulized pre-flashpoint a lot but never in such a cringe worthy or over the top way, even in the new 52 nightwing series I don't remember it being even close as bad as it is in Grayson or Batman and robin eternal and why its bothering me so much is because this is what dc writers are starting to define Dick Grayson as, just that one sexy character, Batman and robin eternal is a perfect example of that. The art for the first half is nice there's nothing special about it but its nice I guess the art for the other half sucks, that's the only word that comes to mind when I think about that art it sucks, its by far not the worst art I've seen from dc though.

Tom King and Tim Seeley's characterization for Dick was what really made me dislike this series, this Dick Grayson isn't Dick at all this. What Tim and Tom did was take fanon Dick Grayson and made him canon,this Dick Grayson is a happy go lucky, airheaded, pushover, who doesn't take anything seriously and is constantly making jokes, can't hold his own in a fight ( which was interesting considering that in one issue he took down a robot, I think it was a robot can't remember, that had powers from members of the justice league yet gets his ass handed to him by pretty much everyone ) and just wants everyone to hold hands and get along, if that's who you think Dick Grayson really is then I don't know what to think except that you've never read a pre-flashpoint comic with Dick Grayson in it and this is pretty much the conclusion I've come to with Tom King and Tim Seeley and I'm convinced that they did their research for Dick on tumblr, his supporting cast isn't that interesting except for midnighter, I really disliked their take on Helena Bertinelli, this series just became a giant mess, I can't even tell you what this series was about because I don't even know anymore, I'm glad that Dick is going to be a hero again in rebirth but I'm not sure if I'm going to be picking up nightwing rebirth considering that Tim Seeley is still going to be writing Dick and it kinds looks like continuation of Grayson in fact I don't think I'm going to be reading another batbook for a while, not until they get new writers on the batbooks, they're pretty much keeping the same group of writers that were on new 52 batbooks for rebirth and these writers just don't understand these characters. I wonder when the real Dick Grayson will return.
rows.title: What a mess

rows.text:
My feelings about this book are incredibly mixed. To start on a positive note, Dick Grayson is my favorite DC character, and the art in Grayson is fantastically beautiful. Mikel Janin is a masterful artist capable of producing stunning visuals. The story has some interesting side-characters I'd like to have seen developed more, with a decent range of diversity including a practicing Muslim spy agent, teenage girls of multiple ethnicities, and a gay anti-hero in its first arc. It had the potential to be an interesting read with an interesting cast. However, with the exceptions of Helen Bertinelli and Agent 1, most of Grayson’s characters (including its titular hero) fail to escape the feeling of one-note plot devices. Unfortunately, it’s all downhill from there.

Theoretically, Grayson is about Dick Grayson working as an agent of Spyral to uncover secrets of the spy organization on behalf of Batman, but we get only a few mentions of this and almost no actual development of it as plot. 90% of Grayson's story in its actuality is comprised of completely gratuitous spy ‘missions’ for Spyral that begin and end within one issue, leaving no build-up or tension continuing from one issue to the next and no feeling of an overall arc or character development. Despite the fact that Dick is surrounded by enemies, trapped (by both writers and extremely dubious, wafer-thin plot logic) into a role he doesn’t fit (and should be hating), cut off from everything and everyone that he loves, with his entire family, friends, and every support network all believing he’s dead, his character shows very little gravitas or emotional depth and almost no sign that any of these things bother him. He simply gads about from mission to vague, unrelated mission as all of the side-characters alternately swoon over and sexually harass him with incredibly awkward and poorly written dialogue.

From a perspective of the spy-genre, the series tries to fill the typical James Bond/Jason Bourne tropes: chases, admiring women, and innuendo galore. However, Dick Grayson himself is not a character particularly well suited to these tropes. The creative team’s efforts to make him fit in a James Bond-esque role result in a character that is not convincing as Dick Grayson -or- James Bond. The writers seem more interested in shoehorning Dick into various honeypot tropes about sexy spies than exploring the actual pre-existing character that so many people already loved.

The biggest downfall of the series is the extent to which the creators concern themselves more with providing a constant flow of over-the-top Dick Grayson fanservice and objectifying dialogue about Dick’s body than telling an actual cohesive story. There’s literally an entire issue devoted to a group of underage girls planting cameras in his room, taking pictures of him almost-naked without his knowledge or consent, oogling them with classy dialogue like “some prime hollywood beefcake, nice bahookie on ‘im too”, chasing him half-naked all over the woods on a “man-ty raid”, and ultimately being rewarded within the story for organizing the sexual harassment and gross invasion of privacy of someone who is, to them, a total stranger. In that issue, out of 18 pages, less than five pages even somewhat advance the series’s already weak plot. The entire rest of the issue is beautifully drawn but incredibly un-Dick Grayson-like fanservice.

These same underage girls later go on to name and discuss each of his ass-cheeks. Literally. On page. Right in front of him. I’ve never seen a more literal or textbook definition of sexual objectification in a comic, and yet the series casts its blatant ongoing sexual harassment as something we should find charming and cute. It’s not. It’s dehumanizing, uncomfortable to read, and incredibly upsetting, especially for people who’ve experienced sexual harassment in reality.

Not only is Dick’s character being suppressed for the sake of trope and titillation in his own series, even outside of the fanservice he’s frequently written with very little agency in his own story events. Dick's character development is frequently put on the backburner in favor of queerbaiting and objectification, to the point where he frequently comes across as very bland, passive, and one-dimensional; an object moved around by the actions of other characters in his own story. Helena Bertinelli, his ‘partner’, is a significantly more active and nuanced character than the title character, probably because in an unusual switch from comics norm, significantly less of her panel time is devoted to showing off her...assets...and put into her character development instead. It frequently feels just as jointly her book as his, because she’s being treated like a nuanced, developed character in all the scenes where Dick Grayson, former Batman, has been relegated to sexy beefcake.

Grayson is an absolutely beautifully drawn and colored book full of intentionally objectifying fanservice and sexual harassment of the main character that comes at the detriment of plot pacing, overall character development, and at the cost of the agency of the primary character. Its plot is negligible and full of holes; gratuitous action that jumps from one trope and catchphrase to the next without any meaningful development or advancement. It’s a series of pretty scenes that seldom move forward into a cohesive overall arc and have nothing to do with the real Dick Grayson or the greater DC world at large. If you’re looking for a Dick Grayson story, dimensional character development, or well-written plot, read something else.
rows.title: Great art; terrible story.  If you want a story about Dick Grayson, look elsewhere.

rows.text:
How many women get bored in their marriage? MANY! So many women get bored with their husbands and Stephanie is no exception.

She truly loves Jacob. But she is bored and he shows no interest at all. She tries everything to get his attention. You name it, she does it. She tries so hard to not cheat on him. But when Derrick comes along and says all the right things and gives her all this attention. How can a girl possibly not give in?!

As things start to heat up, Stephanie finds herself lost in a world full if lust, and loss. The wheels keep turning and there is nothing she can do to stop the train wreck that is about to happen. Only nothing is as it seems. As truths start to surface, there is plenty of heartbreak, loss, lies, and guilt to go around.

Over all, I REALLY enjoyed this book. I felt for Jacob. I hate that the "nice" guy gets cheated on, but I also wanted to slap the shit out of him. I mean seriously, hello, your wife is talking dirty to you, wearing sexy clothes and all you can do is stick it in, cum, and then roll over?! No wonder she cheated.

Things really do heat up in this book and come full circle. I loved it. It was sexy and steamy, as well as nerve wrecking and exciting. Derrick, is sexy, and super hot in bed. Every girls wild fantasy. He's also hiding something major. I totally did not see the ending coming. It was simply a great read.

"Making decisions is easy; it's living with the consequences of our decisions that is hard."

This has to be one of my favorite quotes. And weirdly it's kind I true, good girls like to be dirty in bed. You all know its true even If you won't admit it. Lol.

"....treat a slut like a lady and treat a lady like a slut, and they'll love it. If she's a nice girl, the kind that always does the right thing, treat her like a whore and she'll love it. Don't ask me why, just is what it is."
rows.title: Amazing!

rows.text:
I did like the majority of this book. There are some strong positives, like the author uses great metaphors and similes. (Except strangely she misses the boat a few times and neglects to add the word "like" to some of her sentences. Such as "My heart broke in half"...well NO, it didn't. She should have said "It was like my heart broke in half" or "It felt like my heart broke in half" etc.) Some other positives about the book: the sex is scorching hot and the sexual tension builds throughout the book. (However, some of the author's language is overtly crude and vulgar to the point of making me cringe. There are constant scatological references, and it really took me out of the romantic, sexy frame of mind to have Celian utter the "C" word! That word has no place on anyone's mouth, and it doesn't belong in a romance novel AT ALL (not even a super-sexy one). And speaking of vulgarity, I did not appreciate the constant use of the name Jesus used as a curse word...(some of us still care about that). While I am talking about the negatives for the book, here are a few more. Although the thing about Judith always wearing Converse tennis shoes ("Chucks") is cute, it is unrealistic that she would wear them with a dress to a high-powered job or especially to the charity function she attended!! And although most of the book is edited well, it isn't perfect...I found some sentences with missing words that didn't make sense. And for my last negative criticism, I think the name "Judith Humphries" is a downright hideous name for a romance heroine! The hero has such a romantic name - Celian Laurent, and her name certainly doesn't match that.
Here are some strong positives for the book: the story moves along at a brisk pace, and there are no dead spots or "dragging"...I was never bored. The author uses some very nice details in the book, mentioning food and clothing brands, newspaper names, tv shows and song titles etc. It brings an intimate, realistic tone to the story. (She also includes details specific to her New York City setting). Jude is a likeable character right from the start, and even though she has an immediate one-night stand (we think) with a stranger, she comes off as being sympathetic and not "slutty", because we know she is vulnerable and in emotional pain. As the author intended, Celian comes off as a huge jackass and "man-whore" until much later in the book, when we learn to have sympathy for him and his past. The kinder he is to Jude and the more we see him falling in love with her, the more we readers like Celian. The plot is simple but interesting, and a lot of the peripheral characters are fun or have a quirk that makes them unique in the story. So overall, I liked the book, but it does have several things that drove me nuts (especially the vulgar language!) Just because I enjoy a spicy romance novel doesn't mean I want to read almost constant crassness and vulgarity. Mostly because of this, I would rate this novel at a 3 and 3/4 out of 5 stars.
rows.title: A Flawed Book, But Good For the Most Part

rows.text: The Making of A King really messed with my head, but that's not surprising since N.M. Catalano loves to shake things up in her stories.  Lucas King is a douche, that's all there it to it.  He pissed me off for a very long time in this story, and frankly, I'm not sure that I'll ever get over his questionable decision making because his defense is that he can.  He eats at a diner frequently, where he spies a waitress named Evelyn, aka Evie, and he decides that he wants to make her his to destroy her.  I was like WTH?!  I know, the shady services he provides to people for a living make him all sorts of dark, not to mention those past demons that he keeps locked up.  However, why he wants to destroy Evie kind of escaped me, since she's a ball busting female, who I couldn't love more if I tried.  Of course, that doesn't mean that I didn't have beef with her at first, as I really felt like she was too easy on Lucas, especially when he was so disgusting in my opinion.  I get it, I really do, that she's the light who will take some of his darkness away if she can stand strong against his need for mind games, and/or his man whore ways.  His obsession with her, has him catching feelings really pisses him off, so more Lucas rage/chaos comes in to play.  Of course, this the first part of The King Duet, so you should know that Part 1 ends on an insane cliff hanger.  So, I encourage everyone one click this story which will grasp you in it's dark clutches whether you like Lucas or not!  The next part can't get here soon enough, because I NEED to know if Ms. Catalano will redeem Lucas in my eyes!  HIGHLY HIGHLY HIGHLY RECOMMEND!
rows.title: #TEAMEVIEALLTHEWAY

rows.text:
Jesus tap-dancing Christ... Endless Night, that's what this stupid book is. Just an endless bloody party. Seriously, let me break it down for you:

Chapter 1-4, getting to the stupid party.
Chapter 5-10 getting drunk at the party and being a mega bitch, and pissing off some voodoo priestess.
Chapter 11-12 finally leaving the party. Lots of puking. At some point, she passes out next to some hot guy, and doesn't even get a piece of that hot dick. What the hell
Chapter 13-Whatever... just this stupid fat bitch running around, crying, getting drunk, puking, trying to get away from the voodoo chick. Trying to out-bitch the voodoo bitch, which just ain't gonna happen. I mean, Dylan is a royal bitch, don't get me wrong, but this voodoo bitch has got the serious stink eye.

Then we get to the James Bond bit. You know, the bit where the voodoo bitch lays out her whole plan. She goes on about all the shiat that happened in the first book, The Scene. So, apparently, this voodoo bitch needs skanky whore blood from blond bitches in L.A. to fuel her evil empire. Who knew?

And who the hell cares? Would it be too hard to write another original story with this crazy Dylan bitch, and her trusty side kick Tatum? Why does it have to be the same fake-vampire story, with just a bit of voodoo thrown in? I don't bloody care about the dead cum dumpsters in L.A., I care about this new story in New Orleans, dammit. That's why I'm reading this book. I already read the last book, so I don't need that shiat again.

That being said, it's still a fun book to read. I mean who doesn't want to read about stupid bitches getting drunk and messed up. And, for some reason, I just love reading about Dylan going full-on bitch mode, and getting in everyone's face. It's awesome.

And hell, you just gotta love it when she busts out quips like this one: "Well, bang me with a flip flop!" Ahh Dylan... I hope you never change.

I received a free copy of this book for an honest review.
rows.title: Endless Night - needs to end

rows.text: Pushing Arlo picks up where the first book leaves off, so you really must read Catching London (Book 1) FIRST.  I'm so glad that the talented MV Ellis decided on another book, because Catching London seemed to just drop off, not really a cliffy per se, but just ended so yeah, was super excited to get Pushing Arlo, and it was a great story that really played on my emotions in a shocking way.  See, I really saw Arlo as the spoiled, man whorish rock star, but in this story, I honestly had his back instead of London's to be honest, and that's where my shock came into play.  Where I had London's back since their story began, she really pissed me off with a lot of her decisions in this story, and I'm not quite over them yet.  She was the one who came off badly this time, because honestly, the big twist, aka their big bump in the road, was before they were together, so what she did to him in reaction was just a douche move.  And I had all kinds of colorful metaphors for her, which pissed me off, because we girls are supposed to have the cooler heads in these situations.  Yeah I would give her the time to cool off, but she kept punishing him in ways that really set me off.  So Ms. Ellis obviously had transported me into this story completely, while dragging my extreme emotions out to play, so I can honestly say that I devoured it in one sitting because I had to see how it all panned out!  So if you want to know what happened, and trust me you do, just read this story that I HIGHLY HIGHLY HIGHLY RECOMMEND TO ALL!
rows.title: #SHOCKINGTURNAROUND

rows.text:
Dr. Beans' latest effort "SLUT MONKEY" is now available and well worth the sheckles. Dare I say laugh out loud funny? Yes. Beans once told a bevy of onlookers years ago outside a New York courthouse (paternity case #1a), "I Never met a pun I didn't want to wrestle and get naked with, either order"

The title track has that down home groove, that says what few artists want to ever convey, "I `m a douche bag, you're a total slut, so lets relieve ourselves in the jungle". The well crafted track Rump Roast is a hilarious tale that will leave you craving for some over cooked red meat. The throw away line about tossed salad-awesome! Horace and Boris a feel good song inspired by Cab Calloway and the latest push for legal gay marriage. Body hair a plus in the world of Dr. Beans. I wanted more, what about Richard and Dick? What about Manny and Danny? Hiding in Your Bathroom is an uplifting song showing off the vocal range of Dr. Beans, you just wanted the fuzzy guitar part at the end to be heavier.

Beans has emerged relevant once again. Making people smile, not easy these days, making people laugh, next to impossible. So next time the gas station attendant asks you for $96.00 and asks you what song is playing in your car, tell him its "SLUT MONKEY" Dr. Beans' contribution to keeping spirits high while getting hosed at the pump.
rows.title: wow worth picking up

rows.text:
To be a total douche or not to be a total douche, that is the hottest question. I mean at first glance Ezekiel Daniels is HOT, muscular and just totally dreamy... And hen he speaks. He's like that enigma or the total verbal train wreck you can't seem to look way from or shield your ears from. Because you seriously can't believe the crap coming out of his mouth. Que the eye roll. I mean seriously, who buys into that? Well there is one little lady that has dreams of one Ezekiel, or Zeke, Daniels having some redeeming qualities and she's set on finding them.

Sweet, shy Violet would give anything to be noticed for more than her white blonde hair or stutter. She is almost finished with college and knows exactly where her lonely heart needs to go. Just one problem... While babysitting she runs into the one man she day dreams about and he isn't all peaches and cream like she believes. He gruff and absolutely self centered, something she's not used to. Can he see her inner beauty and fall for her sweetness? Can she see past his douchebaggery and understand the real front he's putting on? These two are the least likely to succeed together and the most rooted for. Love conquers all, but is it enough to bitch slap some sense into a self absorbed ass?

I loved this book for its humor and realism. I mean who can't be behind the damsel and the douche? I was rooting for Zeke to pull his head out of his arse straight away. Even tho is front was brutally gruff, I wanted Violet to be able to melt him into the goo he so fondly made fun of! Cute, endearing, funny and sarcastically brilliant, this book had it all. I was a fan of all the words Sara gave me and couldn't read fast enough. Great story and completely recommend! 4.5 stars-FMR Book Grind
rows.title: He's like that enigma or the total verbal train wreck you ...

rows.text:
They say not to judge a book by it's cover, but damn, when you get a cover like Phenomenal X ^^ you just know that the book is going to be HOT!

I am a fan of a fighter almost as much as I am a fan of a rock star, and Michelle Valentine writes great stories for both.

Xavier "Phenomanal X" Cold is a wrestling star. Recognised everywhere he goes, it's hard to know who wants to know him for him, or who wants to know him for his money.

Anna Cortez, recent college graduate, has lead a sheltered life thanks to her religious upbringing and over protective father.

After a chance meeting, things will never be the same for either of them.

The attraction between them is instant and intense. Talk about spontaneous combustion of underwear!! You can cut the UST with a knife it is so thick!

I love them trying to keep things in the "friend zone". Blind Freddie could see that these two were always going to be more than friends!

The good girl meets bad boy is not a new theme in books these days, but it is the execution of Phenomenal X that made it different for me. Xavier's childhood still plays on his mind, and affects him daily. It was a really shitty childhood and how he has come out of it as well as he has shows his strength.

Anna struggles with what she feels and what she wants, against what she has always been brought up to believe. She is breaking the shackles of her devout Christian family, and she blossoms before our eyes.

At times I wanted to just go to her and say "snap out of it!!" but I could understand her confusion after 21 years of being told what she is feeling is wrong.

When Xavier and Anna finally get their act together ~~ YAY!!!~~ things go from smouldering to smoking hot! These two steam up the pages and burn up the sheets!

There are some pretty horrible secondary characters in Phenomenal X. Xavier's ex-PA Deena is a golddigging whore and a grade A bitch. Every time she was in a scene I wanted to hiss! Rex, another wrestler is just horrible. Out for what he can get, and doesn't care who he tramples on his way to getting it.

But for the bad, there is the good. Nellie and Carl, who took Xavier under their wings when he was a troubled teenager. Brian, another wrestler, and his wife Liz are real. Kind, supportive, true friends to Xavier. And Anna's Aunt Dee and cousin Quinn are wonderful. Just love those two to bits. More family than Anna's family have ever been.

I have been a fan of Michelle Valentine since I first started reading her rock star "Black Falcon" series, and now a fan of her Hard Knock series. She has drawn me into the world of Xavier and Anna, and I don't want to leave.

Oh no you didn't Michelle Valentine! Please please please don't make us wait too long, because that was some cliffhanger you left us with!

Now, while I am waiting for book 2, I am off to YouTube the wrestling!!
rows.title: 4.5 Phenomenal Stars

rows.text:
I'm glad to see you all are finally getting into the Peach...I've read the other reviews of her past discs and you are all CRAZY! Peaches rocks to her fans, not the man, and this album, albeit excellent, feels a bit too soft vocally for me and a bit safe. Don't get me wrong, I dig the Peaches, I think I was her first fan when I heard her music at the Cock in NYC a few years back and she got everyone dancing and groping, but she's getting a bit over produced on the vocals a bit here, sounding a bit whispy like her arobics obsessed fan Madonna (who loves to work out to her). She has this "erotica" quality in her voice which is so mainstream compared to her sexy tough dirty bitch image that put her on the map in the first place, and you have to respect that, no one has pulled this off like her (whomever compared her to Wendy O, please). Check out her live show and you'll see what I mean!

Listen...Buy this album...it rocks! She makes every man wish he had a p*ssy. Really. "Slipery Dick" is great, "Two guys for Every Girl" is a hoot, But Peaches, I loves it when you scream! Not when you're trying to be a teen dream.
rows.title: Nothing wrong with a warm peach

rows.text:
This CD, at first, sounded awfully boring upon first listen, but I gave it a few more chances...after that, there was no turning back! This is a mellow, but catchy and understatedly brilliant CD. Listen to the last 30 seconds of "As You Are" when Fran's voice slowly meets up with the background instruments...it'll give you chills--it's gorgeous.
For me, the big standout tracks are "Writing To Reach You", "The Fear", "Driftwood", and "Why Does It Always Rain On Me". Listen for the very George Harrison sounding guitar solo kick in when you least expect it on "The Fear". I swear it's something that George Harrison would have done with the Beatles.
Really catchy stuff. They don't hit you over the head with it either, like Oasis, you have to pay attention to it and let it seep into your subconscious and you'll be hooked.
Oh, by the way, the "Extra Hidden Track"--Blue Flashing Light--is a rocker which is worth the price of the CD alone! TRUST ME on this one. The whole CD is relatively mellow until 6 minutes and 40 seconds into the last track you hear this beaut fade in and rock your world. "Call me a name and I'll hit you again, you're a slut, you're a bitch, you're a whore." Spewed with such venom! It really doesn't fit into the CD, so I guess that's why it was added way at the end, and I'm glad they did it.
rows.title: Grows on you like crazy!

rows.text:
"After all, that's my brand: racer, hothead, manwhore. My dick -- or my tool, rather -- can't be satiated."

So what do you do when you're in a book funk? You pick up one of Sabrina Paige's Stepbrother romances, of course! ;) I'm really not joking! I've been in a bit of a slump with the books I've been reading lately, and this was a quick, erotic pick-me-up. Still not as taboo as I'd like it to be, but I was liking Tool much better than Prick. Why? Gaige and Delaney were older...right out of college, not high school like the last book, so the sexual relationship was hotter and more believable. Gaige was also Alpha and in charge, but also had a lovable and caring side too. Oh, and the epilogue? So so sweet :-)

I checked out Goodreads and haven't seen book 3 there yet, but I'm really hoping it's in the works! I highly recommend Tool for anyone looking for quick, hot, Alpha read.
rows.title: Quick, Hot, Alpha Read!

rows.text:
Arc received from JA HUSS in exchange for an honest review
4.5 stars

Spencer is a deceiving son of a bitch.

Tramping around town ignoring Veronica, treating her like trash, leaving her high and dry (especially in the alleyway), and breaking her heart by being a callous bastard, he pissed me off! And despite all this, I positively fell in love with the cocky, deceitful phony. Why? Because everything he does is all for his Bombshell. Dig deep down inside, and you’ll find that Spencer is truly a selfless, self-sacrificing man, whose primary concern is the love of his life. He won me over with his unconventional protective ways. But, as they say, the road to hell is paved with good intentions. Spencer will learn his lesson the hard way…

Spencer’s rouse is to keep his distance from Ronnie by casting her out of his life. (Well, except for when he finds the time to have explosive sex). For over a year, Ronnie’s life has been in turmoil because she loves a man who threw her away. His reason? Spencer’s love for Ronnie is so deep, so consuming, he refuses to trap her into his chaotic life. Until all his legal troubles and the trial are over, Ronnie is out of the loop. He vowed never to have his troubles taint her life. In a sick twisted way, Spencer is an honorable guy.

Ronnie is struggling emotionally as well as financially. Her heart is shattered as Spencer constantly denies her what she needs. Just when her life is about to fall apart, a mysterious man swoops in to save the day and Ronnie catches his eye. Hmm… perfect timing. And just the trigger Spencer needed to pull his head out of his butt when it comes to his Bomb. Forcing him to confess his intentions and how he desperately loves her, hoping she is patient enough to wait for the dark clouds looming to float away.

Finally, everything comes to a head. All his plans are shot and everyone is in danger. The story line takes many twists and turns – kicking my anxiety level sky high. Everything Spencer sacrificed unravels leaving everyone, especially Ronnie, knee deep in trouble. BIG TIME DRAMA I never saw coming, involving mysterious characters, created such tense and nail biting moments. I was on the edge on my seat! It was awesome plot twist.

Poor Spencer. In a blink of an eye, everything backfires. He’s left in hell and the realization of what his mistakes cost all of them, especially Ronnie. My heart went out to him as he suffered pain and remorse. His heart was in the right place. However, he is broken and recognizes his sacrifices were unnecessary and wasted precious time by avoiding the love of his life.

Never fear, Spencer and the gang pull out the big GUNS as secrets are revealed and the unthinkable happens. WOW!! Spencer stops at nothing to assure his Bombshell is once again safe, in his arms, and will stay there for eternity.

What I liked about this story were the flashbacks into Ronnie and Spencer’s relationship. Seeing their beginnings and how they fell in love. It was heart warming and arousing. Spencer is totally the “mother@cking boss”. He’s such an incredibly sexy beast. His appetite for the Bombshell was insatiable.

Best part – Epilogue number two. Fan-freaking-tastic. Number Five was adorable. Sweet and snarky. I won’t spoil the surprise. You’ll have to find out who he is on your own. I loved him.

This was a really good series. I will miss this gang of misfits.
rows.title: Spencer is the man

rows.text:
when a series' premise and ensemble add up to the sort of "magic" that outlasts the series itself, it's hard not to be tempted to reunite years later...and just as hard to justify the effort should you succumb to the temptation. it can be done - see Rescue From Gilligan's Island and/or Get Smart Again! - but well, for every one of those, there's at least a dozen misfires like Return Of The Beverly Hillbillies or The Addams Family Halloween Special. in other words, like pretty much everything else in show-business, it's a crapshoot.
it's gratifying and them some to be able to report that the return of Brent Leroy & Company has more in common with Maxwell Smart's triumphant return than with Jed Clampett's pointless rehash. you could perhaps argue that the relative brevity of the gap had something to do with that: where the aforementioned reunions were decades later, this film was designed to commemorate the 10th anniversary of the series' premiere. well, there is indeed room for that possibility, so go ahead and think so if you'd like to.
but don't forget, there were other minefields to navigate their way around as well. by the very nature of movies they had to readjust the formula. the series consisted of 22 1/2-minute episodes which tended to feature three plots per episode, whereas here they had to sustain a single story for 95 minutes. even the fact that it was predominately the same crew - producer Virginia Thompson, producer/director David Storey - couldn't guarantee that it would work out. but then, it didn't hurt, either.
the story finds Dog River, that realm that appears to be in the prairies, feeling as much more like The Twilight Zone as ever. well-meaning but ditzy mayor "Fitzy" (Cavan Cunningham) has made a number of less than astute investments which left the town on the verge of bankruptcy. even the local power and water are only sporadically working. the obvious class of panic ensues, and the situation isn't helped much by the fact that our central core of characters have their personal issues to deal with as well.
take the police force. when Davis (Lorne Cardinal) is forced to retire he decides to be a private detective a' la Sam Spade, and sets up shop in the police station basement. so Karen (Tara Spencer-Nairn) finds herself keeping the peace single-handedly...which would be tricky enough if she weren't pregnant. it's mentioned in passing that she has a husband stationed "somewhere on the Mediterranean coast." in a textbook example of the series' surrealist spirit, they simply acknowledge with a shrug that Spencer-Nairn happened to be pregnant during production rather than contriving some backstory that would only of bogged down the pace.
the old folks' find excuses in the situation to be as prickly as ever. father Oscar (Eric Peterson) sets out to become a wilderness survivalist and trades the car in for a horse. mother Emma (Janet Wright) tries to recognize the severity of the crisis but can't help being more preoccupied with the fact that Brent (Brent Butt) is still single and, slightly more to the point, she still has no grandchildren.
Hank (Fred Ewanuick) is, alas, still the same few I.Q. points shy of qualifying for the post of Village Idiot. this time he tries to get a doughnut shop chain to open a local branch, which obviously strains his relationship with Ruby café proprietor Lacey (Gabrielle Miller). any "dope" character could do this much of course, but it takes a Hank go one better (or worse): he makes a garbled and disjointed attempt to enlist Brent's support in some plan he has, which he assumes Brent will bankroll, and gets pissed off when he doesn't.
Wanda (Nancy Robertson), the local intellectual - whom, you'll recall, got more and more morally felxible as the series progressed - finds the obligatory angle to exploit. asked to housesit while Davis goes on vacation, she coverts his "man cave" garage into a bar and/or casino.
Lacey is the one with the most free brain cells to devote to the crisis. her plan is to spruce what's left of the town up just enough to win a "quaintest town in Canada" contest, but she actually alienates people by throwing too much of her passion into the cause. criticized for being "bossy," she replies "because i'm the boss." at the point where things are their darkest she is ousted in favor of Emma. usually Emma is the ideal choice for such a committee, but as already mentioned, at this point she's too preoccupied with Brent's biological clock to give it her all.
which leaves Brent, who turns out to be the character who develops here. (that's the advantage of writing the movie you're starring in.) the usually indolent gas-station owner tends to pride himself on his ability to "sit things out." but his "happy place" is in more trouble that he can hide from himself this time, and he makes an effort by buying and trying to run the abandoned local bar. he seems to be limping along pretty well, but then comes a confrontation with the aforementioned doughnut chain's representative, who is injured in his attempt to leave the premises. reformed know-it-all Wanda offers her services as attorney, but even she can't get around the fact that Brent is, technically, guilty.
so we have a few substantial messes to clear up here, and needless to say our heroes manage to sort things out. what's especially unique is that they manage to present as maudlin an ultimate resolution without being maudlin in the least. as per the unique spirit of the unique series, they keep from being sappy by going for the sap whole-hog. like the classic series in which Adam West plays Batman, they spoof established conventions by playing them absolutely to the hilt.
all of which builds up to a truly astonishing twist. actually two, if you count the fact it took until that final scene for Oscar's catchphrase, "jackass!," to make it into the film. (who knew they could even DO that?)
and let us not forget that because this is a film we get a few surprises for the upped ante. for instance, the film begins by finally showing the suggested showdown between a werewolf and a robot.
the most significant, though, is that we finally get to see Wullerton. one of the outstanding running-gags of the series is that the denizens of Dog River have, for no readily apparent reason, such disdain for the neighboring town of Wullerton that everyone spits on the ground whenever it's mentioned. and we finally get to see why it's so horrible: where Dog River is a community of dysfuctional, flaws humans, Wullerton is as pure, wholesome, goody-two-shoes a place as you could possibly imagine. they even held fundraisers to help Dog River out of it's crisis, "the self-serving bastards."
they say to always go out on top and leave an audience wanting more. as of November of 2014, Corner Gas has done so not once, but twice!
rows.title: Welcome Back To Dog River. (while it lasts.)

rows.text:
"It was sad, but a fact of life in this country--the women were at the mercy of the men, and often the men were anything but merciful."

Gabe is a soldier, stationed in Afganistan. He has a sexual relationship with a local widow, Natalya. As a widow with no husband to support her, Natalya is forced into a life of prostitution. While Gabe is completely up-front with her, that all he wants is sex, she grows to love him. Gabe cares for her. You can see it in his jealousy of her other men--in how he takes care of her in the little ways that he is able to--fixing things up around the house and bringing her things like blankets and chocolates.

"...having sex with her got more and more difficult when I knew there were emotions involved--her love, and my guilt."

Gabe ends things with Natalya. While most will find him to be a complete ass for doing so, I loved Gabe from the start. He reminds me SO much of my guy friends from high school. Gabe was honest with her from beginning. He was not looking for love. But, like most women, Natalya let her emotions take over. As women, we seek out what men try to avoid, love and commitment.

Just after breaking things off with his "whore," a group of Human Rights inspectors shows up with a bunch of college interns to check out the conditions in the area. Gabe gets buddied up with Alexis. Alexis is a spitfire and is not impressed by Gabe's "charm." She calls him on his shit and rather than turning him off, her feistiness only makes him want her more.

"Does that crap work for you... the whole `Oh babe, let's get busy, you and me.' Are there girls in the world who find that attractive? Do you get laid with lines like that?"
"Yeah, it works for me... and you know what I think... I think it works for you too...You're afraid you won't be able to control a guy like me, aren't you? And you know what? You wouldn't be able to control me. But I'd take you on the ride of your life, and you'd thank me for it afterwards."

Holy smokes!!! Gabe *fans self* Gabe... *fans self again* I cannot believe this sexy man came out of the mind of the sweet little lady, you see below. He is such a GUY and I love that. His character is my absolute favorite thing about Camouflaged. While he is a womanizing, smart-ass that thinks with his dick first; Gabe is also a lover and a protector. He is brave and selfless and when he and Alexis find themselves in a hopeless situation, Gabe is a soldier through and through. He is a hero.

Camouflaged blew me away. I was surprised at how well developed the characters were and how amazing the story was for a novella. A lot of authors don't master this in full novels two and three times the size of Camouflaged. Well done Selena!!! I am anxiously awaiting being reunited with my new book boyfriend in Concealed.

Read this book ladies!! Gabe will make your toes curl! And it is only .99! You have no excuses... get the book... read it... love it... and fan-girl all over the lovely Selena Laurence!!! She is amazing!
rows.title: LOVE Gabe!!

rows.text: I LOVED this book! Yes, it took me a while to read it but I've personally been busy in my personal life. But I give this TWO thumbs up! Amanda is smart, snarky, sarcastic and has THREE rules (read the book if you want to know the rules). Shane is a CrossFit coach, he's arrogant, snarky, sexy, and into Amanda. The back-and-forth commentary between these two are noteworthy. Shane's friend and CrossFit coach Kate gets the insecure bitch vote from me. I totally did NOT like her. Kyle...he's sexy and if he were my friend and did what he did I would have given him the arse**** bomb too. I'm not into being blindsided by other people's meddling and that's one of the MANY reasons why Kate gets the 'B' vote. I understand people wanted the best for her but sometimes people need to work and sort out their own issues and that's exactly what Amanda did. Oh I forgot to mention Amanda, Steph, and Danielle boss Master Bader is a complete douche.
rows.title: I LOVED this book

rows.text:
Rats piss me off, so i bought the 6 pack and now im LOOKING for more rats to kill. Works great and no blood splatter. Had this smartass little shit eating the food and not setting off the trap so I improvised. I placed the trap on top of a sticky mouse trap pad and worked wonders.
I use my dog food as bait with a little bit of peanut butter to let the dog food sink into it. They go for it ALL THE TIME.
Need a professional rat exterminator?
Give me a call!
rows.title: Finally i can sleep!

rows.text:
Well butter my butt and call me a biscuit" this book is outstanding!
This book was gloriously thrilling and twisted. And I absolutely LOVED it!
I never read thrillers or books like this but as I love Lauren's writing style I just had to give it a go!

Buttercup is a perfectly twisted psychotic woman, who despite some of her actions and insane plans you can't help but love her! The way Lauren writes her is brilliant, it was as though I could actually hear her talking at points! She was totally hilarious! Despite being slightly crazy Buttercup is loveable and vulnerable and at points I really felt for her.

The other characters in this book are amazing and all play a great role in the story, not once did I sit and think "ok so what relevance did they have"
Wesley takes the whole "controlling possessive alpha" to a whole other level, maybe a whole other planet!

I love Lauren's wit and humour which pours out in droves during this book. Some one liners made me howl with laughter! And I couldn't have described the husband any better if I'd tried (Porno-King-Lying-Cheating-Bastard-Motherfucker-Sack-of-Shit-Husband-of-Mine-Kurtis-Asshole-Jackman)

I loved the reference to The Club, that was genius!

The constant twists and turns in this book kept me flipping the pages like a woman possessed. I couldn't read it fast enough just to see what was going to happen. And the last couple of chapters, holy wow! Didn't see it coming at all!

This is a mega 5 star read for me! I'll recommend it to anyone! Lauren you are amazing!
rows.title: OUTSTANDING!!!!

rows.text:
Oh WOW! Thrown in at the deep end, I was again hooked and up all night devouring this ‘unputdownable’ book which I had been impatiently waiting for. My kindle was down to emergency power rations but even it was so engrossed it hung on so I could finish it!

This is the third volume in the fabulously gritty, raw and dirty but definitely sex and lust filled diary of Bink Cummings. You must read (and if you haven’t yet why the heck not?) first two volumes before diving into this third fantastic book.

OK, where do I start? Well Big was his usual loving a-hole self but this time he also gave another side to him, a sensitive and vulnerable side that came out when he realised how far he had pushed Bink, the love of his life that he needed to share the reasons behind some of his behaviours. So whilst in some parts I was spewing all the cuss words and more at him, in others I was all gushy and ‘aww Big, you sweet thang’

Bink is still holding back from falling right in and moving her and Big’s relationship to another level. This of course set Big off on a route to push her to reveal her true feelings to him. So both are looking for a sign from each other that will escort them down that happy little effin path to fully commit but as always with this kick-ass pair they take us on one hell of an emotive ride with them!

It was good that Bink had Gunz supporting her all the way as this time Big just pushed things a little too far with his effed up rationalisation about BJs and whores (still makes my blood boil thinking about that part!) Then off Big goes on a two week secret ride with the brothers, leaving Gunz and Deke to hold the fort – well we then get some other revelations about these two. How did I not see what Deke was hiding? I love this guy hard and he needs some serious loving (I volunteer) As for Gunz – well I might not agree with his ‘lady’ selection and where he does his business but my word he is one kinky bastard. Love him!

There were a lot of revelations of the past, especially in relation to Bink’s biatch of a mother and what a freakin’ curve ball she threw into the mix! But it also made me understand Bink’s Dad more for some weird reason. Anyhow not revealing that, nope that would be one huge spoilerism!

I loved the touching moments between Bink and Big, especially where he talks to the ‘bump’ Harley. So friggin’ sweet as eff. But as always Big just made me squeal with his macho, all-encompassing alpha, caveman attitude that had me swooning, swearing and shouting at the kindle! Yes he makes me do a lot of things that begin with S! As for Bink, well you just got to love her HARD. This woman has been through so much but still she strides through life with the strength of an ox, not giving a beep, she oodles respect to be given. This pair are a match made in heaven and sometimes hell but one thing is for certain – the sparks fly! The chemistry and love just sizzles and draws the reader in. I am raring every time to sit on that bitch seat watching everything fly. I almost feel as if I am there in on the action (apart from Gunz in the bathroom…ahem)

As always the secondary characters are amazing and the Sacred Sisters are hilarious. I loved the visit to the Spa, especially Gunz sat there with them getting some treatments. Got to love the man. I loved to see how Beth has come out of her shell and I was surprised at the gumption she gave out when someone who shall remain nameless tried to treat her badly. To the kerb man, to the kerb!

I absolutely loved the epilogue and can’t wait to read BIG the next book in the chronicles. I am already imagining what is going to fly out of his mouth. Oh and in the wings I hope we get Deke’s story? With…..ME? *Gasps* Well a woman can hope!

Bink has done it again and given a fabulously outrageous insight to her world and I for one am so stoked I get to be in on this fantastic ride. Now I need therapy!
Five zillion stars.
rows.title: Unputdownable!!

rows.text:
STUCK IN BETWEEN illustrates a love-triangle at its best; one where everyone can have their cake and eat it too... well, almost everyone!

MEET JACQS: 28 YO SW/F, A green-eyed, brunette looking for a relationship OUTSIDE her current group of friends... That's tough, especially when that group of friends is the one you tend to spend all your free time - that kind of makes it nearly impossible to "fish outside the pond" - you know, when you never LEAVE the pond?

MEET BOND: 35 YO SW/M, 5'10" tall, With longish brown hair and light brown eyes, he's a handsome man, but not necessarily one that would stop you dead in your tracks kind of handsome. Apparently his body produces some ferocious pheromones, because the ladies love him and he just can't seem to say, "No" to them. He fishes in "the pond" too, but he also visits everyone else's pond! He's got it bad for Jacques, but he has very little will power when it comes to also "dating" other women and always seems to mess things up with Jacqs - but those damn pheromones are just irresistible.

❝Simply, I was crazy horny and hanging out with a past lover, specifically the one with the most magnificent cock I ever had the pleasure of experiencing, did not help matters.❞

MEET RED: 35 YO SW/M, 6'2" tall Well, they call him Red for a reason, so I will let you guess that one ☺ Red's a born entertainer and he is definitely in "the pond". He is also Bond's BFF. He and Jacques have always had this love/hate thing going on, but of course you've probably guessed that they were just using the "hate" part as an excuse to avoid the attraction and chemistry they so obviously share.

So, what's the problem? The problem is that Jacqs soon finds herself STUCK IN BETWEEN two sexy men and for once, it's her turn to have her cake and eat it too!

❝When will you understand that my dating other women doesn't take away from my love for you?❞

GAH! Bond! What an idiot! Like that would actually fly with 98% of the women in the world... This makes him sound like a douche-bag, but he really isn't - I actually adored him once he got is big-ole-head out of his arse. He just has some major issues he needs to work through.

Red, on the other hand, has his s*** together and he knows what he wants. He's dominant, driven and loyal to the core, but sometimes in life, you just have to dive in headfirst and think about the consequences later.

❝Afraid of breaking the spell that I knew would end once reality set in, I sat, waiting. He ran his hands up my neck, followed by his mouth, kissing and nibbling. Chills fluttered down my spine and I moaned, "Oh, Red." Wetness pooled in my panties and I wanted him to take me. I wanted to know what his cock looked like, tasted like. I didn't care if we ever spoke again, or if my motivations stemmed from getting back at Bond. Red had sparked an intense need and I wanted him to take me.❞

When the trio finally comes up with a solution to their love-triangle (about ¾ of the way into the book), traditional rules get thrown out the window and the result is B-O-O-M! OFF THE CHARTS!

STUCK IN BETWEEN is all about complicated relationships and all the drama that ensues. It's a ménage read, but it is so much more than that and I totally loved it. This is a great book for those who are "old pros" and have read loads of ménage themed books and it's an awesome book for those looking to check out the ménage scene for the first time. Author Blakely Bennett is one of my favorites, so I am not shocked that I loved this book - I just hope you all love it as much as I did! Go and get it and tell the world!

✳✳ Copy provided by the author/publisher for an honest review.
rows.title: B-O-O-M! OFF THE CHARTS GOOD!

rows.text:
My-my-my this book was hawt! Best friends realizing that they're actually in love with each other? Ah, now those are the kinds of romances that can be sweet, passionate, painful, and just make a reader's toes curl in pleasure. Which is exactly what this book does! J Lynn (AKA Jennifer L. Armentrout) is an amazing writer. She's wonderful at weaving her magic writing and hooking readers into the story. Frigid is no different, man it will keep you up late into the night!

Sydney is your average bookworm, with a serious up tight attitude. She's always on time, gets stellar grades, and is a bit of a goody two-shoes. (Is anyone else noticing how often Armentrout's female leads are bookworms . . . fan pandering.) Kyler is goooooooood looking and quite the smooth talker. One look at him and you want him, then he opens his mouth and you've got'sta have! These two have amazing chemistry. Man I was just flaming up!

Yet, despite all the heat, passion, romance, some sweet moments, and laughs . . . their was anger and quite a bit of indignation. Kyler is a man whore. Sure the author throws in some shiny moments where he takes a girl to his place, but he does not sleep with her. However, he's a man whore enough that Syd thinks he can't keep his dick out of any woman. But underneath all of that he's only trying to hide his unrequited love for Syd. Which is funny because he has this huge revelation towards the end that he loves Syd. Bam, it hits him. But Armentrout tries to have moments where Kyler is suppressing something concerning Syd . . .

In reality, even though I'd been with everyone else, I'd always been Syd's.

Then there's Syd. Right from the get go readers know that she has unrequited love for Kyler. Every man whore thing he does hurts her. Everyone can see this. (Heck, everyone can see how Kyler just wants her for himself.) Not only is she a bookworm, but she's only really had one boyfriend (that I suspect she was trying to use to camouflage her feeling for Kyler) and of course it wasn't good. The one time they had sex was a doozy. No matter how piggy Kyler is she doesn't want to jeopardize their friendship. It's like self abuse! He means too much so she can't tell him how she feels because their friendship would end. Obviously she's not worthy (and yes Kyler doesn't think he's worthy of her) and then she just knows Kyler is such a slut that he could not handle that kind of relationship with a woman. Even when he royally fugs up this is what her best-friend says:

“Guys are totally stupid when it comes to unrequited love. We females pine away and keep our thighs closed for the most part when we love someone we can't have. Guys swing their shit around at anything that has a hole, trying to forget the one they want.”

I shouldn't read too much into it (ha-ha) and should just enjoy the show. But it irks me! He's so in love with her that he screws everyone else! What the hell! If you love someone soooooooo much that you're on a self destructive path and you actually put up with all that crap, TELL THEM HOW YOU FEEL before they get a sexually transmitted disease. Also, how could they both not notice how in love the other person was with them. If they know each other so well it doesn't take a rocket scientist. I wanted to read about Sydney dating someone else and moving on because I thought Kyler was such a schmuck. I'm tired of that excuse in the quote above. The scenario was unbelievable. It was painful at times. Maybe I'm one of the few who felt that way, but I just get tired of all the crap. Why couldn't Syd have had a wonderful relationship before with amazing sex! Why does she have to basically set herself up in a horrid relationship to get abused because she can't have her man. Yes, they both think that by stepping up their relationship they will ruin they're friendship.

OK-OK! All that unbelievable drama is what makes this book addicting, and others I've liked. But it was too much. Kyler needed to have his dick whacked, not in the good way. So, this book will definitely make you feel intense things. Roller-coaster of insane emotions! Weeeeeee! If you're not touchy about the things mentioned above you'll just love this book. Or maybe you'll enjoy the passion her so much that you'll be able to ignore it for the most part. Despite how much I found certain parts infuriating, it was a great book! No really. It was awesome.

Sexual Content: New Adult with steam scenes. This ain't Young Adult, o-ho-o-ho-no! Sex my people, lot's of hot-to-trot sex.

3/5- Adored it, just a few minor details held it back.

Originally posted at Book Whispers.
rows.title: So hot! Yet super annoying.

rows.text:
I judge a book not only from the feelings it gives me while reading it but also how it stays with me after I have to stop reading to go back to real life. While doing real world stuff the books is still in my head the whole time.
What is he going to do next?
What sweet or douchey word is going to come out of his mouth?
How is she going to react?
When is she going to give in or stand up to the prick?

Pricks like Jesse Ward, Gideon Cross, Colton Donovan, Christian Grey, Damien Stark, Lucien Knight.
Sweethearts like Jake Wethers, Jeremiah Jordan, Ethan Blackstone, Remington Tate and Travis Maddox are just a few that stayed in my head after I put my kindle down.
Well I have another to add to my list- Cane Alexander. He is the guy that Adriana Locke has allowed us to meet. The more I read the more I fall in love with him and hate that I have to put the kindle down to rejoin the world. At first he is the cocky shit that causes me to drool but then he develops into a sweet heart that I love.

rows.title: Tracklisting

rows.text:
Two films, 2 discs, 2 versions, 2 pterosaurs beasts, and 2 hairy-ass monsters. Toho the studio that brought us Godzilla/Gojira, Baran/Varan, Baragon, King Ghidorah or Ghidrah, Manda, Dogora or Dagora, Gigan, and a ton of other monsters brings as War of the Gargantuas and Rodan. It's true what they say Toho is the giant monster factory or Daikaiju factory. For those who aren't familiar with giant monster films or aren't die hard fans like myself, daikaiju is the Japanese term for giant monster.

WAR OF THE GARGANTUAS (1966)

Story: Japanese version: The cells that broke away from the Frankenstein monster in Frankenstein Conquers the World/Frankenstein vs Baragon (1965) have mutated into giant Bigfoot-like behemoths. Sanda (the brown Gargantua) is peaceful, loving, and kind. Gaira (G-eye-rah) (the green Gargantua)is mean, viscous, cruel, and violent basically a John McCain monster just kidding. Sanda discovers that Gaira has been killing and consuming people, so a big battle is under way. My 3 favorite scenes are #3: Sanda beats Gaira with a tree. #2: Gaira vs Odako a giant octopus. #1: Sanda & Gaira's final fight.

The Good: Great score, acting is good except for that singing bitch who has words stuck in her throat and Russ Tamblyn who is talented but acted like a dick while filming and hardly put forth any effort. Great writing, great effects, great story, good dialogue, great pacing, and great film.

The Bad: An annoying singer who thankfully gets eaten by Gaira, which is the only non-evil thing Gaira ever does. Russ Tamblyn puts forth no effort. The print quality is very dark which is not understandable since all other Japanese prints had good quality.

Original Japanese version: 4.8/5 stars

English dubbed version: Sanda and Gaira are just two Bigfoot-like monsters that are brothers who hate each other for the reasons stated above. In this version all references to Frankenstein are cut from the film.

The Good: Everything good mentioned above. The print quality is great.

The Bad: Most of Ifukube's original score is replaced. Erased elements of original story. Russ Tamblyn still acts like a prick who gave no effort and that annoying singer is still there.

English Dubbed version: 3/5 stars

Disc comes with no extra features. WAR OF THE GARGANTUAS disc gets 5/5 stars.

Fun Facts: The original ending of the film was to not only have Sanda and Gaira swallowed up by the new volcano, but the lava was to have spread to Tokyo where it was to destroy the city as well as the remaining cells of the monsters. US producer Henry G. Saperstein had planned to make a sequel where either Sanda, Gaira or a similar, new creature were pitted against Godzilla. It was called Godzilla vs. the Gargantuas but the project was never set in motion.In Crank 2 (2009) there is a fight scene which is an homage to the final battle scene in The War of the Gargantuas--it takes place in a transformer plant and utilizes many of the cinematic cues from the Gargantuas film. The Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated episode "Battle of the Humungonauts" is a parody/homage to this film, going so far as to reference the song "Feel in My Heart (The Words Get Stuck in my Throat)" which appeared in the film. Actor Brad Pitt cited the movie as his inspiration to go into acting at the 84th Academy Awards.

RODAN (1956)

Story: Japanese version: Two prehistoric eggs hatch revealing 2 Rodans. The Rodans feed off of giant insects called Meganula and soon feed upon humans. The eggs were discovered in a mine as well as the big bugs.

The Good: Great score, great effects, great characters, great story, great acting, great dialogue, and over all great film. Print quality looks great.

Original Japanese version: 5/5 stars

English dubbed version: Same story but dubbed in English with cut and added scenes.

The Good: Same from Japanese version. Print quality is okay.

The Bad: Cuts scenes, added in cliched scenes, and most of the score is missing.

English dubbed version: 3/5 stars

Disc contains new documentary entitled bring Godzilla down to size. Rodan disc gets 5/5 stars.

Fun Facts: Veteran writer Ken Kuronuma, who wrote the original story for this film, was inspired by an incident in Kentucky in 1948, when Captain Thomas F. Mantell, a pilot for the Kentucky Air National Guard, died in a crash while allegedly pursuing a UFO.
While shooting the scene in which Rodan flies over the bridge in Saikai Village in Kyushu, the pulley from which Haruo Nakajima was suspended broke. He fell from a height of twenty-five feet, but the wings and the water, which was about one and a half feet deep, absorbed much of the impact. The giant insects featured in this film, the Meganula, would later go on to appear in the 2000 film Godzilla vs. Megaguirus. In It by Stephen King, the boy Mike is haunted by the image of Rodan. Mike sees a giant bird (the clown Pennywise) in the ruins of a steel mill and thinks of Rodan. This was the first Toho monster movie shot in color.

Note: When I saw Rodan at the age of 6 the ending made me cry.

WAR OF THE GARGANTUAS disc content:
Japanese version
English version
subtitles

Rodan disc content:
Japanese version
English version
subtitles
Documentary

VERDICT: BUY IT! Rodan & War of the Gargantuas are both solid kaiju films and both I love.
rows.title: Horror, Sci-Fi, Drama, and action thrown into 2 films.

rows.text:
In Eye of the Burning Man, when child pornographers kidnap Mick's maid's young nephew, and a crack whore asks Mick for protection, he is happy to leave the studio to mix it up with the lowlifes---but what if he's being set up for a lethal case of revenge?

Here we have the 2nd "Mick Callahan" book, Mick is on the up and becoming very successful but trouble is not too far away for our Mick!
While leaving a radio studio late at night, Mick is attacked by a masked man and gets dumped by his girlfriend for his troubles. To add more misery his maids young nephew is abducted. Is that it? No, a girl called Mary asks Mick for help from her nasty pimp, of course it's time for Mick too kick some ass!

Along the way Mick requires help from Darlene (a cop) and not forgetting Jerry (a computer expert) and Hal (Mick's sponsor who has plenty of money)All the clues lead to Nevada's Burning Man Festival and a confrontation even Mick was not expecting.....

This 2nd Callahan novel is probably my favourite, Harry Shannon brings plenty scuffs and scrapes for Mick and I like it! This is a fast read because it is that good, mystery/noir is becoming something Harry Shannon is very good at. All the cool characters are here to enjoy, whether they be good or bad.

I only hope more people in the UK pick up some of Harry Shannon's books, he really deserves a bigger audience.
rows.title: Mick Callahan returns to kick ass!!!
